revision.text:
The {{nihongo|'''Takamatsuzuka Tomb'''|高松塚古墳|''Takamatsuzuka Kofun''}}or "Tall Pine Tree Ancient Burial Mound" in Japanese is an ancient [[tomb]] of circular shape located in [[Asuka]] [[village]], [[Nara prefecture]], [[Japan]].
The tomb is thought to have been built at some time between the end of the 7th century and the beginning of the 8th century, but was accidentally discovered by a local farmer in the 1960s.
The mound of the tomb was built up in alternating layers of [[clay]] and [[sand]]. It is about 16 meters in diameter and 5 meters in height. Digging yielded a burial chamber with beautifully-painted [[fresco]] wall paintings of [[courtier]]s in [[Korea]]n-style garb. The paintings are in full color with red, blue, gold, and silver foil representing four male followers and four abigails together with the Azure Dragon, Black Warrior, White Tiger, and [[constellation]]s of stars. The tomb is believed to be for a Korean [[noble]] who migrated to Japan.
The [[Cultural Affairs Agency]] of Japan is considering taking apart the stone chamber and reassembling it elsewhere to prevent further deterioration to its wall paintings. A painting called Asuka Bijin, or "beautiful women", is one of the [[mural]]s in the tomb facing deterioration. The unusual preservation method is being considered because the tomb's current situation makes it impossible to prevent further damage and stop the spread of [[mold]].
Unlike with the Kitora tomb, also in Asuka, removing pieces of the Takamatsuzuka wall [[plaster]] and reinforcing them for conservation appears difficult because the plaster has numerous tiny cracks.
===External links=== *[http://gias.snu.ac.kr/wthong/publication/paekche/eng/hi3-5.pdf Paekche Culture in Asuka Japan]
==See also== *[[History of Japan]]
[[Category:Japan]]

Digging yielded a burial chamber with beautifully-painted [[fresco]] wall paintings of [[courtier]]s in [[Korea]]n-style garb. The paintings are in full color with red, blue, gold, and silver foil representing four male followers and four abigails together with the Azure Dragon, Black Warrior, White Tiger, and [[constellation]]s of stars. The tomb is believed to be for a Korean [[noble]] who migrated to Japan.
The [[Cultural Affairs Agency]] of Japan is considering taking apart the stone chamber and reassembling it elsewhere to prevent further deterioration to its wall paintings. A painting called Asuka Bijin, or "beautiful women", is one of the [[mural]]s in the tomb facing deterioration. The {{nihongo|'''Takamatsuzuka Tomb'''|高松塚古墳|''Takamatsuzuka Kofun''}} or "Tall Pine Tree Ancient Burial Mound" in [[Japanese language|Japanese]] is an ancient [[tomb]] of circular shape located in [[Asuka]] [[village]], [[Nara prefecture]], [[Japan]].
The tomb is thought to have been built at some time between the end of the 7th century and the beginning of the 8th century, but was accidentally discovered by a local farmer in the 1960s.
The mound of the tomb was built up in alternating layers of [[clay]] and [[sand]]. It is about 16 meters in diameter and 5 meters in height. Digging yielded a burial chamber with beautifully-painted [[fresco]] wall paintings of [[courtier]]s in [[Korea]]n-style garb. The paintings are in full color with red, blue, gold, and silver foil representing four male followers and four abigails together with the Azure Dragon, Black Warrior, White Tiger, and [[constellation]]s of stars. The tomb is believed to be for a Korean [[noble]] who migrated to Japan.
The [[Cultural Affairs Agency]] of Japan is considering taking apart the stone chamber and reassembling it elsewhere to prevent further deterioration to its wall paintings. A painting called Asuka Bijin, or "beautiful women", is one of the [[mural]]s in the tomb facing deterioration. The unusual preservation method is being considered because the tomb's current situation makes it impossible to prevent further damage and stop the spread of [[mold]].
Unlike with the Kitora tomb, also in Asuka, removing pieces of the Takamatsuzuka wall [[plaster]] and reinforcing them for conservation appears difficult because the plaster has numerous tiny cracks.
==External links== *[http://gias.snu.ac.kr/wthong/publication/paekche/eng/hi3-5.pdf Paekche Culture in Asuka Japan]
==See also== *[[History of Japan]]
[[Category:Japan]] [[ja:高松塚古墳]]

The {{nihongo|'''Takamatsuzuka Tomb'''|高松塚古墳|''Takamatsuzuka Kofun''}} or "Tall Pine Tree Ancient Burial Mound" in [[Japanese language|Japanese]] is an ancient [[tomb]] of circular shape located in [[Asuka]] [[village]], [[Nara prefecture]], [[Japan]].
The tomb is thought to have been built at some time between the end of the 7th century and the beginning of the 8th century, but was accidentally discovered by a local farmer in the 1960s.
The mound of the tomb was built up in alternating layers of [[clay]] and [[sand]]. It is about 16 meters in diameter and 5 meters in height. Digging yielded a burial chamber with beautifully-painted [[fresco]] wall paintings of [[courtier]]s in [[Korea]]n-style garb. The paintings are in full color with red, blue, gold, and silver foil representing four male followers and four abigails together with the Azure Dragon, Black Warrior, White Tiger, and [[constellation]]s of stars.
For whom the tomb is built is unknown, but the decorations suggest it is for a member of royal family or a high-ranking nobleman. Candidates include: Prince Osakabe (? - 705), a son of [[Emperor Temmu]], Prince Yuge (? - 699), also a son of Emperor Temmu, Isonokami Ason Maro (640 - 717), who is a descendant of [[Mononobe clan]] and in charge of Fujiwara-kyo after the capital was moved to [[Heijo Palace|Heijo-kyo]].
The [[Cultural Affairs Agency]] of Japan is considering taking apart the stone chamber and reassembling it elsewhere to prevent further deterioration to its wall paintings. A painting called Asuka Bijin, or "beautiful women", is one of the [[mural]]s in the tomb facing deterioration. The unusual preservation method is being considered because the tomb's current situation makes it impossible to prevent further damage and stop the spread of [[mold]].
Unlike with the Kitora tomb, also in Asuka, removing pieces of the Takamatsuzuka wall [[plaster]] and reinforcing them for conservation appears difficult because the plaster has numerous tiny cracks.
==External links== *[http://gias.snu.ac.kr/wthong/publication/paekche/eng/hi3-5.pdf Paekche Culture in Asuka Japan]
==See also== *[[History of Japan]]
[[Category:Japan]] [[ja:高松塚古墳]]

The [[Cultural Affairs Agency]] of Japan is considering taking apart the stone chamber and reassembling it elsewhere to prevent further deterioration to its wall paintings. The tomb is thought to have been built at some time between the end of the 7th century and the beginning of the 8th century, but was accidentally discovered by a local farmer in the 1960s.
The mound of the tomb was built up in alternating layers of [[clay]] and [[sand]]. It is about 16 meters in diameter and 5 meters in height. Candidates include: Prince Osakabe (? - 705), a son of [[Emperor Temmu]], Prince Yuge (? - 699), also a son of Emperor Temmu, Isonokami Ason Maro (640 - 717), who is a descendant of [[Mononobe clan]] and in charge of Fujiwara-kyo after the capital was moved to [[Heijo Palace|Heijo-kyo]], [[Kudara no Konikishi|Kudara no Konikishi Zenko]] (617-700), a son of the last king of [[Baekje]].[http://www.asukanet.gr.jp/asukahome/ASUKA2/TAKAMATUTUKA/takamatutuka.html][http://www.nara-shimbun.com/special/takamatu/vol_02d_01.html]
The [[Cultural Affairs Agency]] of Japan is considering taking apart the stone chamber and reassembling it elsewhere to prevent further deterioration to its wall paintings. A painting called Asuka Bijin, or "beautiful women", is one of the [[mural]]s in the tomb facing deterioration. The {{nihongo|'''Takamatsuzuka Tomb'''|高松塚古墳|''Takamatsuzuka Kofun''}} or "Tall Pine Tree Ancient Burial Mound" in [[Japanese language|Japanese]] is an ancient [[tomb]] of circular shape located in [[Asuka]] [[village]], [[Nara prefecture]], [[Japan]].
The tomb is thought to have been built at some time between the end of the 7th century and the beginning of the 8th century, but was accidentally discovered by a local farmer in the 1960s.
The mound of the tomb was built up in alternating layers of [[clay]] and [[sand]]. It is about 16 meters in diameter and 5 meters in height. Digging yielded a burial chamber with beautifully-painted [[fresco]] wall paintings of [[courtier]]s in [[Korea]]n-style garb. The paintings are in full color with red, blue, gold, and silver foil representing four male followers and four abigails together with the [[Azure Dragon (Chinese constellation)|Azure Dragon]], [[Black Tortoise (Chinese constellation)|Black Tortoise]] [[White Tiger (Chinese constellation)|White Tiger]], and [[Chinese constellation]]s of stars.
For whom the tomb is built is unknown, but the decorations suggest it is for a member of the Japanese royal family or a high-ranking nobleman. Candidates include:
:1. Prince Osakabe (? - 705), a son of [[Emperor Temmu]] :2. Prince Yuge (? - 699), also a son of Emperor Temmu :3. Isonokami Ason Maro (640 - 717), who is a descendant of [[Mononobe clan]] and in charge of Fujiwara-kyo after the capital was moved to [[Heijo Palace|Heijo-kyo]] :4. [[Kudara no Konikishi|Kudara no Konikishi Zenko]] (617-700), a son of the last king of [[Baekje]]. [http://www.asukanet.gr.jp/asukahome/ASUKA2/TAKAMATUTUKA/takamatutuka.html][http://www.nara-shimbun.com/special/takamatu/vol_02d_01.html]
The [[Cultural Affairs Agency]] of Japan is considering taking apart the stone chamber and reassembling it elsewhere to prevent further deterioration to its wall paintings. A painting called Asuka Bijin, or "beautiful women", is one of the [[mural]]s in the tomb facing deterioration. The unusual preservation method is being considered because the tomb's current situation makes it impossible to prevent further damage and stop the spread of [[mold]].
Unlike with the [[Kitora Tomb]], also in Asuka, removing pieces of the Takamatsuzuka wall [[plaster]] and reinforcing them for conservation appears difficult because the plaster has numerous tiny cracks.
==External links== *[http://gias.snu.ac.kr/wthong/publication/paekche/eng/hi3-5.pdf Paekche Culture in Asuka Japan]

The unusual preservation method is being considered because the tomb's current situation makes it impossible to prevent further damage and stop the spread of [[mold]].
Unlike with the [[Kitora Tomb]], also in Asuka, removing pieces of the Takamatsuzuka wall [[plaster]] and reinforcing them for conservation appears difficult because the plaster has numerous tiny cracks.

For whom the tomb is built is unknown, but the decorations suggest it is for a member of the Japanese royal family or a high-ranking nobleman. Candidates include:
# Prince Osakabe (? - 705), a son of [[Emperor Temmu]] # Prince Yuge (? - 699), also a son of Emperor Temmu # Isonokami Ason Maro (640 - 717), a descendant of [[Mononobe clan]] and in charge of Fujiwara-kyo after the capital was moved to [[Heijo Palace|Heijo-kyo]] # [[Kudara no Konikishi|Kudara no Konikishi Zenko]] (617-700), a son of the last king of [[Baekje]]. The [[Cultural Affairs Agency]] of Japan is considering taking apart the stone chamber and reassembling it elsewhere to prevent further deterioration to its wall paintings. A painting called Asuka Bijin, or "beautiful women", is one of the [[mural]]s in the tomb facing deterioration. The unusual preservation method is being considered because the tomb's current situation makes it impossible to prevent further damage and stop the spread of [[mold]].
Unlike with the [[Kitora Tomb]], also in Asuka, removing pieces of the Takamatsuzuka wall [[plaster]] and reinforcing them for conservation appears difficult because the plaster has numerous tiny cracks.

revision.comment: Revert to revision 78129597 dated 2006-09-27 16:44:16 by Tortfeasor using [[:en:Wikipedia:Tools/Navigation_popups|popups]]
revision.text:
The {{nihongo|'''Takamatsuzuka Tomb'''|高松塚古墳|''Takamatsuzuka Kofun''}} or "Tall Pine Tree Ancient Burial Mound" in [[Japanese language|Japanese]] is an ancient [[tomb]] of circular shape located in [[Asuka]] [[village]], [[Nara prefecture]], [[Japan]].
The tomb is thought to have been built at some time between the end of the 7th century and the beginning of the 8th century, but was accidentally discovered by a local farmer in the 1960s.
The mound of the tomb was built up in alternating layers of [[clay]] and [[sand]]. It is about 16 meters in diameter and 5 meters in height. Digging yielded a burial chamber with beautifully-painted [[fresco]] wall paintings of [[courtier]]s in [[Korea]]n-style garb. The paintings are in full color with red, blue, gold, and silver foil representing four male followers and four abigails together with the [[Azure Dragon (Chinese constellation)|Azure Dragon]], [[Black Tortoise (Chinese constellation)|Black Tortoise]], [[White Tiger (Chinese constellation)|White Tiger]], and [[Chinese constellation]]s of stars.
For whom the tomb is built is unknown, but the decorations suggest it is for a member of the Japanese royal family or a high-ranking nobleman. Candidates include:
# Prince Osakabe (? - 705), a son of [[Emperor Temmu]] # Prince Yuge (? - 699), also a son of Emperor Temmu # Isonokami Ason Maro (640 - 717), a descendant of [[Mononobe clan]] and in charge of [[Fujiwara-kyo]] after the capital was moved to [[Heijo Palace|Heijo-kyo]] # [[Kudara no Konikishi|Kudara no Konikishi Zenko]] (617-700), a son of the last king of [[Baekje]], one of the [[Three Kingdoms of Korea]]. [http://www.asukanet.gr.jp/asukahome/ASUKA2/TAKAMATUTUKA/takamatutuka.html][http://www.nara-np.co.jp/special/takamatu/vol_02d_01.html]
The [[Cultural Affairs Agency]] of Japan is considering taking apart the stone chamber and reassembling it elsewhere to prevent further deterioration to its wall paintings. A painting called Asuka Bijin, or "beautiful women", is one of the [[mural]]s in the tomb facing deterioration. The unusual preservation method is being considered because the tomb's current situation makes it impossible to prevent further damage and stop the spread of [[mold]].
Unlike with the [[Kitora Tomb]], also in Asuka, removing pieces of the Takamatsuzuka wall [[plaster]] and reinforcing them for conservation appears difficult because the plaster has numerous tiny cracks.
==External links== *[http://www.pref.nara.jp/nara/kaido/eg/ko_nara/spot/k05.htm Takamatsuzuka ancient burial mound] *[http://www.japantimes.co.jp/weekly/news/nn2005/nn20050702a5.htm The Japan Times Weekly Online - Takamatsuzuka tomb to be moved] *[http://gias.snu.ac.kr/wthong/publication/paekche/eng/hi3-5.pdf Paekche Culture in Asuka Japan]
==See also== *[[History of Japan]]
[[Category:Archaeological sites in Japan]] [[Category:Classical Japan]]

A painting called Asuka Bijin, or "beautiful women", is one of the [[mural]]s in the tomb facing deterioration. The unusual preservation method is being considered because the tomb's current situation makes it impossible to prevent further damage and stop the spread of [[mold]].
Unlike with the [[Kitora Tomb]], also in Asuka, removing pieces of the Takamatsuzuka wall [[plaster]] and reinforcing them for conservation appears difficult because the plaster has numerous tiny cracks.

[[Image:Asuka Byako.JPEG|thumb|180px|This beautiful mural painting of the Takamatsuzuka Tomb depicts [[White Tiger (Chinese constellation)|Byakko]], the White Tiger]]
The {{nihongo|'''Takamatsuzuka Tomb'''|高松塚古墳|''Takamatsuzuka Kofun''}} or "Tall Pine Tree Ancient Burial Mound" in [[Japanese language|Japanese]] is an ancient [[tomb]] of circular shape located in [[Asuka]] [[village]], [[Nara prefecture]], [[Japan]].
The tomb is thought to have been built at some time between the end of the 7th century and the beginning of the 8th century, but was accidentally discovered by a local farmer in the 1960s.
The mound of the tomb was built up in alternating layers of [[clay]] and [[sand]]. It is about 16 meters in diameter and 5 meters in height. Digging yielded a burial chamber with beautifully-painted [[fresco]] wall paintings of [[courtier]]s in [[Korea]]n-style garb. The paintings are in full color with red, blue, gold, and silver foil representing four male followers and four abigails together with the [[Azure Dragon (Chinese constellation)|Azure Dragon]], [[Black Tortoise (Chinese constellation)|Black Tortoise]], [[White Tiger (Chinese constellation)|White Tiger]], and [[Chinese constellation]]s of stars. The paintings are disgnated as a [[National treasures of Japan|national treasure of Japan]].
For whom the tomb is built is unknown, but the decorations suggest it is for a member of the Japanese royal family or a high-ranking nobleman. Candidates include:
# [[Prince Osakabe]] (? - 705), a son of [[Emperor Temmu]] # [[Prince Yuge]] (? - 699), also a son of Emperor Temmu # [[Prince Takechi]] (654? - 696), also a son of Emperor Temmu,general of [[Jinshin War]],Daijō Daijin # Isonokami Ason Maro (640 - 717), a descendant of [[Mononobe clan]] and in charge of [[Fujiwara-kyo]] after the capital was moved to [[Heijo Palace|Heijo-kyo]] # [[Kudara no Konikishi|Kudara no Konikishi Zenko]] (617-700), a son of the last king of [[Baekje]], one of the [[Three Kingdoms of Korea]]. [http://www.asukanet.gr.jp/asukahome/ASUKA2/TAKAMATUTUKA/takamatutuka.html][http://www.nara-np.co.jp/special/takamatu/vol_02d_01.html]
The [[Cultural Affairs Agency]] of Japan is considering taking apart the stone chamber and reassembling it elsewhere to prevent further deterioration to its wall paintings. A painting called Asuka Bijin, or "beautiful women", is one of the [[mural]]s in the tomb facing deterioration. The unusual preservation method is being considered because the tomb's current situation makes it impossible to prevent further damage and stop the spread of [[mold]].
Unlike with the [[Kitora Tomb]], also in Asuka, removing pieces of the Takamatsuzuka wall [[plaster]] and reinforcing them for conservation appears difficult because the plaster has numerous tiny cracks.
==External links== *[http://www.pref.nara.jp/nara/kaido/eg/ko_nara/spot/k05.htm Takamatsuzuka ancient burial mound] *[http://www.japantimes.co.jp/weekly/news/nn2005/nn20050702a5.htm The Japan Times Weekly Online - Takamatsuzuka tomb to be moved] *[http://gias.snu.ac.kr/wthong/publication/paekche/eng/hi3-5.pdf Paekche Culture in Asuka Japan]

# [[Prince Osakabe]] (? - 705), a son of [[Emperor Temmu]] # [[Prince Yuge]] (? - 699), also a son of Emperor Temmu # [[Prince Takechi]] (654? - 696), also a son of Emperor Temmu,general of [[Jinshin War]],Daijō Daijin # Isonokami Ason Maro (640 - 717), a descendant of [[Mononobe clan]] and in charge of [[Fujiwara-kyo]] after the capital was moved to [[Heijo Palace|Heijo-kyo]] # [[Kudara no Konikishi|Kudara no Konikishi Zenko]] (617-700), a son of the last king of [[Baekje]], one of the [[Three Kingdoms of Korea]]. The unusual preservation method is being considered because the tomb's current situation makes it impossible to prevent further damage and stop the spread of [[mold]].

[[Image:Asuka Byako.JPEG|thumb|This mural painting of the Takamatsuzuka Tomb depicts [[White Tiger (Chinese constellation)|Byakko]], the White Tiger]] [[Image:Takamatsuzuka tomb mural.jpg|thumb|[[Takamatsuzuka Tomb]] mural.]] The {{nihongo|'''Takamatsuzuka Tomb'''|高松塚古墳|''Takamatsuzuka Kofun''}} or "Tall Pine Tree Ancient Burial Mound" in [[Japanese language|Japanese]] is an ancient circular [[tomb]] in [[Asuka, Nara|Asuka village]], [[Nara prefecture]], [[Japan]].
The tomb is thought to have been built at some time between the end of the 7th century and the beginning of the 8th century. It was accidentally discovered by a local farmer in the 1960s.
The mound of the tomb was built of alternating layers of [[clay]] and [[sand]]. It is about 16 meters in diameter and 5 meters high. Digging yielded a burial chamber with painted [[fresco]] wall paintings of [[courtier]]s in [[Goguryeo]]-style garb. The paintings are in full color with red, blue, gold, and silver foil representing four male followers and four abigails together with the [[Azure Dragon (Chinese constellation)|Azure Dragon]], [[Black Tortoise (Chinese constellation)|Black Tortoise]], [[White Tiger (Chinese constellation)|White Tiger]], and [[Vermilion Bird (Chinese constellation)|Vermilion Bird]] groups of stars. The paintings are disgnated as a [[National treasures of Japan|national treasure of Japan]].
For whom the tomb was built is unknown, but the decorations suggest it is for a member of the Japanese royal family or a high-ranking nobleman. Candidates include:
# [[Prince Osakabe]] (? - 705), a son of [[Emperor Temmu]] # [[Prince Yuge]] (? - 699), also a son of Emperor Temmu # [[Prince Takechi]] (654? - 696), also a son of Emperor Temmu, general of [[Jinshin War]], [[Daijō Daijin]] # Isonokami Ason Maro (640 - 717), a descendant of [[Mononobe clan]] and in charge of [[Fujiwara-kyo]] after the capital was moved to [[Heijo Palace|Heijo-kyo]] # [[Kudara no Konikishi|Kudara no Konikishi Zenko]] (617-700), a son of the last king of [[Baekje]], one of the [[Three Kingdoms of Korea]]. [http://www.asukanet.gr.jp/asukahome/ASUKA2/TAKAMATUTUKA/takamatutuka.html][http://www.nara-np.co.jp/special/takamatu/vol_02d_01.html]

The paintings are in full color with red, blue, gold, and silver foil representing four male followers and four abigails together with the [[Azure Dragon (Chinese constellation)|Azure Dragon]], [[Black Tortoise (Chinese constellation)|Black Tortoise]], [[White Tiger (Chinese constellation)|White Tiger]], and [[Vermilion Bird (Chinese constellation)|Vermilion Bird]] groups of stars. The paintings are designated as a [[National Treasures of Japan|national treasure of Japan]].
For whom the tomb was built is unknown, but the decorations suggest it is for a member of the Japanese royal family or a high-ranking nobleman. Candidates include:
# [[Prince Osakabe]] (? - 705), a son of [[Emperor Temmu]] # [[Prince Yuge]] (? - 699), also a son of Emperor Temmu # [[Prince Takechi]] (654? - 696), also a son of Emperor Temmu, general of [[Jinshin War]], [[Daijō Daijin]] # Isonokami Ason Maro (640 - 717), a descendant of [[Mononobe clan]] and in charge of [[Fujiwara-kyo]] after the capital was moved to [[Heijo Palace|Heijo-kyo]] # [[Kudara no Konikishi|Kudara no Konikishi Zenko]] (617-700), a son of the last king of [[Baekje]], one of the [[Three Kingdoms of Korea]]. [http://www.asukanet.gr.jp/asukahome/ASUKA2/TAKAMATUTUKA/takamatutuka.html][http://www.nara-np.co.jp/special/takamatu/vol_02d_01.html]
The [[Cultural Affairs Agency]] of Japan is considering taking apart the stone chamber and reassembling it elsewhere to prevent further deterioration to its wall paintings. A painting called Asuka Bijin, or "beautiful women", is one of the [[mural]]s in the tomb facing deterioration. The unusual preservation method is being considered because the tomb's current situation makes it impossible to prevent further damage and stop the spread of [[mold]].
Unlike the [[Kitora Tomb]], also in Asuka, removing pieces of the Takamatsuzuka wall [[plaster]] and reinforcing them for conservation appears difficult because the plaster has numerous tiny cracks.

==History== The tomb is thought to have been built at some time between the end of the 7th century and the beginning of the 8th century. It was accidentally discovered by a local farmer in the 1960s.
==Description== The mound of the tomb was built of alternating layers of [[clay]] and [[sand]]. It is about 16 meters in diameter and 5 meters high. Excavation yielded a burial chamber with painted [[fresco]] wall paintings of [[courtier]]s in [[Goguryeo]]-style garb. The paintings are in full color with red, blue, gold, and silver foil representing four male followers and four abigails together with the [[Azure Dragon (Chinese constellation)|Azure Dragon]], [[Black Tortoise (Chinese constellation)|Black Tortoise]], [[White Tiger (Chinese constellation)|White Tiger]], and [[Vermilion Bird (Chinese constellation)|Vermilion Bird]] groups of stars. The paintings are [[Cultural Properties of Japan|designated]] a [[National Treasures of Japan|National Treasure]].
For whom the tomb was built is unknown, but the decorations suggest it is for a member of the Japanese royal family or a high-ranking nobleman. Candidates include:
# [[Prince Osakabe]] (? - 705), a son of [[Emperor Temmu]] # [[Prince Yuge]] (? - 699), also a son of Emperor Temmu # [[Prince Takechi]] (654? - 696), also a son of Emperor Temmu, general of [[Jinshin War]], [[Daijō Daijin]] # Isonokami Ason Maro (640 - 717), a descendant of [[Mononobe clan]] and in charge of [[Fujiwara-kyo]] after the capital was moved to [[Heijo Palace|Heijo-kyo]] # [[Kudara no Konikishi|Kudara no Konikishi Zenko]] (617-700), a son of the last king of [[Baekje]], one of the [[Three Kingdoms of Korea]]. [http://www.asukanet.gr.jp/asukahome/ASUKA2/TAKAMATUTUKA/takamatutuka.html][http://www.nara-np.co.jp/special/takamatu/vol_02d_01.html]
==Conservation== The [[Cultural Affairs Agency]] of Japan is considering taking apart the stone chamber and reassembling it elsewhere to prevent further deterioration to its wall paintings. A painting called ''Asuka Bijin'', or "beautiful women", is one of the [[mural]]s in the tomb facing deterioration. The unusual preservation method is being considered because the tomb's current situation makes it impossible to prevent further damage and stop the spread of [[mold]].
Unlike the [[Kitora Tomb]], in Asuka removing pieces of the Takamatsuzuka wall [[plaster]] and reinforcing them for conservation appears difficult because the plaster has numerous tiny cracks.
==Comparison== In 2012, similar mural was found in a tomb in [[Mongolia]]. The round mound, thought to be built by [[Göktürks]] in the 7th century, was excavated by Mongolian and Kazakh researchers. The mural depicts Azure Dragon and White Tiger with a procession of Chinese, [[Sogdiana]] and Caucasoid traders.
==See also== *[[Detachment of wall paintings]] *[[List of National Treasures of Japan (paintings)]] *[[List of Special Places of Scenic Beauty, Special Historic Sites and Special Natural Monuments]] *[[Asuka-Fujiwara]]
==External links== *[http://www.pref.nara.jp/nara/kaido/eg/ko_nara/spot/k05.htm Takamatsuzuka ancient burial mound] *[http://www.japantimes.co.jp/weekly/news/nn2005/nn20050702a5.htm The Japan Times Weekly Online - Takamatsuzuka tomb to be moved] *[http://gias.snu.ac.kr/wthong/publication/paekche/eng/hi3-5.pdf Paekche Culture in Asuka Japan]

Excavation yielded a burial chamber with painted [[fresco]] wall paintings of [[courtier]]s in [[Goguryeo]]-style garb. The paintings are in full color with red, blue, gold, and silver foil representing four male followers and four abigails together with the [[Azure Dragon (Chinese constellation)|Azure Dragon]], [[Black Tortoise (Chinese constellation)|Black Tortoise]], [[White Tiger (Chinese constellation)|White Tiger]], and [[Vermilion Bird (Chinese constellation)|Vermilion Bird]] groups of stars. The paintings are [[Cultural Properties of Japan|designated]] a [[National Treasures of Japan|National Treasure]].
For whom the tomb was built is unknown, but the decorations suggest it is for a member of the Japanese royal family or a high-ranking nobleman. Candidates include:
# [[Prince Osakabe]] (? - 705), a son of [[Emperor Temmu]] # [[Prince Yuge]] (? - 699), also a son of Emperor Temmu # [[Prince Takechi]] (654? - 696), also a son of Emperor Temmu, general of [[Jinshin War]], [[Daijō Daijin]] # Isonokami Ason Maro (640 - 717), a descendant of [[Mononobe clan]] and in charge of [[Fujiwara-kyo]] after the capital was moved to [[Heijo Palace|Heijo-kyo]] # [[Kudara no Konikishi|Kudara no Konikishi Zenko]] (617-700), a son of the last king of [[Baekje]], one of the [[Three Kingdoms of Korea]]. [http://www.asukanet.gr.jp/asukahome/ASUKA2/TAKAMATUTUKA/takamatutuka.html][http://www.nara-np.co.jp/special/takamatu/vol_02d_01.html]
==Conservation== The [[Cultural Affairs Agency]] of Japan is considering taking apart the stone chamber and reassembling it elsewhere to prevent further deterioration to its wall paintings. A painting called ''Asuka Bijin'', or "beautiful women", is one of the [[mural]]s in the tomb facing deterioration. The unusual preservation method is being considered because the tomb's current situation makes it impossible to prevent further damage and stop the spread of [[mold]].
Unlike the [[Kitora Tomb]], in Asuka removing pieces of the Takamatsuzuka wall [[plaster]] and reinforcing them for conservation appears difficult because the plaster has numerous tiny cracks.
==Comparison== In 2012, similar mural was found in a tomb in [[Mongolia]]. The round mound, thought to be built by [[Göktürks]] in the 7th century, was excavated by Mongolian and Kazakh researchers. The mural depicts Azure Dragon and White Tiger with a procession of Chinese, [[Sogdiana]] and Caucasoid traders.
==See also== *[[Detachment of wall paintings]] *[[List of National Treasures of Japan (paintings)]] *[[List of Special Places of Scenic Beauty, Special Historic Sites and Special Natural Monuments]] *[[Asuka-Fujiwara]]
==External links== {{commonscat|Takamatsuzuka_Kofun|Takamatsuzuka Tomb}} *[http://www.pref.nara.jp/nara/kaido/eg/ko_nara/spot/k05.htm Takamatsuzuka ancient burial mound] *[http://www.japantimes.co.jp/weekly/news/nn2005/nn20050702a5.htm The Japan Times Weekly Online - Takamatsuzuka tomb to be moved] *[http://gias.snu.ac.kr/wthong/publication/paekche/eng/hi3-5.pdf Paekche Culture in Asuka Japan] * [http://www.nabunken.go.jp/asuka/index.html Asuka Historical Museum]{{ja icon}}
{{coord|34.4623|135.8062|type:landmark_region:JP|display=title}}

[http://www.asukanet.gr.jp/asukahome/ASUKA2/TAKAMATUTUKA/takamatutuka.html][http://www.nara-np.co.jp/special/takamatu/vol_02d_01.html]

==History== The tomb is thought to have been built at some time between the end of the 7th century and the beginning of the 8th century. It was accidentally discovered by a local farmer in the 1960s.
==Description== The mound of the tomb was built of alternating layers of [[clay]] and [[sand]]. It is about 16 meters in diameter and 5 meters high. Excavation yielded a burial chamber with painted [[fresco]] wall paintings of [[courtier]]s in [[Goguryeo]]-style garb. The paintings are in full color with red, blue, gold, and silver foil representing four male followers and four abigails together with the [[Azure Dragon (Chinese constellation)|Azure Dragon]], [[Black Tortoise (Chinese constellation)|Black Tortoise]], [[White Tiger (Chinese constellation)|White Tiger]], and [[Vermilion Bird (Chinese constellation)|Vermilion Bird]] groups of stars. For whom the tomb was built is unknown, but the decorations suggest it is for a member of the Japanese royal family or a high-ranking nobleman. Candidates include:
# [[Prince Osakabe]] (d. 705), a son of [[Emperor Tenmu]] # [[Prince Yuge]] (d. 699), also a son of Emperor Tenmu # [[Prince Takechi]] ({{circa|654}} – 696), also a son of Emperor Temmu, general of [[Jinshin War]], [[Daijō Daijin]] # Isonokami Ason Maro (640–717), a descendant of [[Mononobe clan]] and in charge of [[Fujiwara-kyō]] after the capital was moved to [[Heijō-kyō]] # [[Kudara no Konikishi|Kudara no Konikishi Zenko]] (617–700), a son of the last king of [[Baekje]], one of the [[Three Kingdoms of Korea]]. [[Image:Takamatsuzuka Asuka Bijin.jpg|thumb|right|0px|One of the "Asuka Bijin"]] The {{nihongo|'''Takamatsuzuka Tomb'''|高松塚古墳|''Takamatsuzuka Kofun''}} or "Tall Pine Tree Ancient Burial Mound" in [[Japanese language|Japanese]] is an ancient circular [[tomb]] in [[Asuka, Nara|Asuka village]], [[Nara Prefecture]], [[Japan]].
==History== The tomb is thought to have been built at some time between the end of the 7th century and the beginning of the 8th century. It was accidentally discovered by a local farmer in the 1960s.
==Description== The mound of the tomb was built of alternating layers of [[clay]] and [[sand]]. It is about 16 meters in diameter and 5 meters high. Excavation yielded a burial chamber with painted [[fresco]] wall paintings of [[courtier]]s in [[Goguryeo]]-style garb. The paintings are in full color with red, blue, gold, and silver foil representing four male followers and four abigails together with the [[Azure Dragon (Chinese constellation)|Azure Dragon]], [[Black Tortoise (Chinese constellation)|Black Tortoise]], [[White Tiger (Chinese constellation)|White Tiger]], and [[Vermilion Bird (Chinese constellation)|Vermilion Bird]] groups of stars. The paintings are [[Cultural Properties of Japan|designated]] a [[National Treasures of Japan|National Treasure]].
For whom the tomb was built is unknown, but the decorations suggest it is for a member of the Japanese royal family or a high-ranking nobleman. Candidates include:
# [[Prince Osakabe]] (d. 705), a son of [[Emperor Tenmu]] # [[Prince Yuge]] (d. 699), also a son of Emperor Tenmu # [[Prince Takechi]] ({{circa|654}} – 696), also a son of Emperor Temmu, general of [[Jinshin War]], [[Daijō Daijin]] # Isonokami Ason Maro (640–717), a descendant of [[Mononobe clan]] and in charge of [[Fujiwara-kyō]] after the capital was moved to [[Heijō-kyō]] # [[Kudara no Konikishi|Kudara no Konikishi Zenkō]] (617–700), a son of the last king of [[Baekje]], one of the [[Three Kingdoms of Korea]]. [http://www.asukanet.gr.jp/asukahome/ASUKA2/TAKAMATUTUKA/takamatutuka.html][https://web.archive.org/web/20070907112805/http://www.nara-np.co.jp/special/takamatu/vol_02d_01.html]
==Conservation== The [[Cultural Affairs Agency]] of Japan is considering taking apart the stone chamber and reassembling it elsewhere to prevent further deterioration to its wall paintings. A painting called ''Asuka Bijin'', or "beautiful women", is one of the [[mural]]s in the tomb facing deterioration. The unusual preservation method is being considered because the tomb's current situation makes it impossible to prevent further damage and stop the spread of [[mold]].
Unlike the [[Kitora Tomb]], in Asuka removing pieces of the Takamatsuzuka wall [[plaster]] and reinforcing them for conservation appears difficult because the plaster has numerous tiny cracks.
==Comparison== In 2012, similar mural was found in a tomb in [[Mongolia]]. The round mound, thought to be built by [[Göktürks]] in the 7th century, was excavated by Mongolian and Kazakh researchers. The mural depicts Azure Dragon and White Tiger with a procession of Chinese and [[Sogdians|Sogdian]], and other Caucasoid traders.
==See also== *[[Detachment of wall paintings]] *[[List of National Treasures of Japan (paintings)]] *[[List of Special Places of Scenic Beauty, Special Historic Sites and Special Natural Monuments]] *[[Asuka-Fujiwara]]
==External links== {{commonscat|Takamatsuzuka_Kofun|Takamatsuzuka Tomb}} *[https://web.archive.org/web/20110305022936/http://www.pref.nara.jp/nara/kaido/eg/ko_nara/spot/k05.htm Takamatsuzuka ancient burial mound] *[http://www.japantimes.co.jp/weekly/news/nn2005/nn20050702a5.htm The Japan Times Weekly Online - Takamatsuzuka tomb to be moved] *[https://web.archive.org/web/20060503114824/http://gias.snu.ac.kr/wthong/publication/paekche/eng/hi3-5.pdf Paekche Culture in Asuka Japan] * [http://www.nabunken.go.jp/asuka/index.html Asuka Historical Museum]{{ja icon}}

revision.comment: /* Description */ Changed "Abigail" to "serving maiden," because very few modern English speakers would recognize Abigail's meaning.

[[Image:Takamatsuzuka Asuka Bijin.jpg|thumb|right|0px|One of the "Asuka Bijin"]] The {{nihongo|'''Takamatsuzuka Tomb'''|高松塚古墳|''Takamatsuzuka Kofun''}} or "Tall Pine Tree Ancient Burial Mound" in [[Japanese language|Japanese]] is an ancient circular [[tomb]] in [[Asuka, Nara|Asuka village]], [[Nara Prefecture]], [[Japan]].
==History== The tomb is thought to have been built at some time between the end of the 7th century and the beginning of the 8th century. It was accidentally discovered by a local farmer in the 1960s.
==Description== The mound of the tomb was built of alternating layers of [[clay]] and [[sand]]. It is about {{convert|16|m}} in diameter and {{convert|5|m}} high. Excavation yielded a burial chamber with painted [[fresco]] wall paintings of [[courtier]]s in [[Goguryeo]]-style garb. The paintings are in full color with red, blue, gold, and silver foil representing four male followers and four serving maidens together with the [[Azure Dragon (Chinese constellation)|Azure Dragon]], [[Black Tortoise (Chinese constellation)|Black Tortoise]], [[White Tiger (Chinese constellation)|White Tiger]], and [[Vermilion Bird (Chinese constellation)|Vermilion Bird]] groups of stars. The paintings are [[Cultural Properties of Japan|designated]] a [[National Treasures of Japan|National Treasure]].
For whom the tomb was built is unknown, but the decorations suggest it is for a member of the Japanese royal family or a high-ranking nobleman. Candidates include:
# [[Prince Osakabe]] (d. 705), a son of [[Emperor Tenmu]] # [[Prince Yuge]] (d. 699), also a son of Emperor Tenmu # [[Prince Takechi]] ({{circa|654}} – 696), also a son of Emperor Temmu, general of [[Jinshin War]], [[Daijō Daijin]] # Isonokami Ason Maro (640–717), a descendant of [[Mononobe clan]] and in charge of [[Fujiwara-kyō]] after the capital was moved to [[Heijō-kyō]] # [[Kudara no Konikishi|Kudara no Konikishi Zenkō]] (617–700), a son of the last king of [[Baekje]], one of the [[Three Kingdoms of Korea]].<ref>[http://www.asukanet.gr.jp/asukahome/ASUKA2/TAKAMATUTUKA/takamatutuka.html]</ref><ref>[https://web.archive.org/web/20070907112805/http://www.nara-np.co.jp/special/takamatu/vol_02d_01.html]</ref>
==Conservation== The [[Cultural Affairs Agency]] of Japan is considering taking apart the stone chamber and reassembling it elsewhere to prevent further deterioration to its wall paintings. A painting called ''Asuka Bijin'', or "beautiful women", is one of the [[mural]]s in the tomb facing deterioration. The unusual preservation method is being considered because the tomb's current situation makes it impossible to prevent further damage and stop the spread of [[Mold (fungus)|mold]].
Unlike the [[Kitora Tomb]], in Asuka removing pieces of the Takamatsuzuka wall [[plaster]] and reinforcing them for conservation appears difficult because the plaster has numerous tiny cracks.
==Comparison== In 2012, similar mural was found in a tomb in [[Mongolia]]. The round mound, thought to be built by [[Göktürks]] in the 7th century, was excavated by Mongolian and Kazakh researchers. The mural depicts Azure Dragon and White Tiger with a procession of Chinese and [[Sogdians|Sogdian]], and other Caucasoid traders.
==See also== *[[Detachment of wall paintings]] *[[List of National Treasures of Japan (paintings)]] *[[List of Special Places of Scenic Beauty, Special Historic Sites and Special Natural Monuments]] *[[Asuka-Fujiwara]]
==References== {{Reflist}}
==External links== {{commonscat|Takamatsuzuka_Kofun|Takamatsuzuka Tomb}} *[https://web.archive.org/web/20110305022936/http://www.pref.nara.jp/nara/kaido/eg/ko_nara/spot/k05.htm Takamatsuzuka ancient burial mound] *[http://www.japantimes.co.jp/weekly/news/nn2005/nn20050702a5.htm The Japan Times Weekly Online - Takamatsuzuka tomb to be moved] *[https://web.archive.org/web/20060503114824/http://gias.snu.ac.kr/wthong/publication/paekche/eng/hi3-5.pdf Paekche Culture in Asuka Japan] * [http://www.nabunken.go.jp/asuka/index.html Asuka Historical Museum]{{in lang|ja}}
{{coord|34.4623|135.8062|type:landmark_region:JP|display=title}}
{{Authority control}}

revision.text:
[[File:高松塚古墳.jpg|thumb|Takamatsuzuka Tomb]] The {{nihongo|'''Takamatsuzuka Tomb'''|高松塚古墳|''Takamatsuzuka Kofun''}} is an ancient {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular tomb|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} in [[Asuka, Nara|Asuka village]], [[Nara Prefecture]], [[Japan]].
==History== The tomb is thought to have been built at some time between the end of the 7th century and the beginning of the 8th century. It was accidentally discovered by a local farmer in the 1960s. [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 3]][[Category:qq 3]][[en:Takamatsuzuka Tomb]][[Category:kk]] ==Description== [[File:Takamat1.jpg|thumb|A mural of women on the western wall of the tomb]] The mound of the tomb was built of alternating layers of [[clay]] and [[sand]]. It is about {{convert |16|m}} in diameter and {{convert|5|m}} high. Excavation yielded a burial chamber with painted [[fresco]] wall paintings of [[courtier]]s in [[Goguryeo]]-style garb. The paintings are in full color with red, blue, gold, and silver foil representing four male followers and four serving maidens together with the [[Azure Dragon (Chinese constellation) |Azure Dragon]], [[Black Tortoise (Chinese constellation)|Black Tortoise]], [[White Tiger (Chinese constellation)|White Tiger]], and [[Vermilion Bird (Chinese constellation)|Vermilion Bird]] groups of stars. The paintings are [[Cultural Properties of Japan|designated]] a [[National Treasures of Japan|National Treasure]].
For whom the tomb was built is unknown, but the decorations suggest it is for a member of the Japanese royal family or a high-ranking nobleman. Candidates include:
# [[Prince Osakabe]] (d. 705), a son of [[Emperor Tenmu]] # [[Prince Yuge]] (d. 699), also a son of Emperor Tenmu # [[Prince Takechi]] ({{circa|654}} – 696), also a son of Emperor Temmu, general of [[Jinshin War]], [[Daijō Daijin]] # Isonokami Ason Maro (640–717), a descendant of [[Mononobe clan]] and in charge of [[Fujiwara-kyō]] after the capital was moved to [[Heijō-kyō]] # [[Kudara no Konikishi|Kudara no Konikishi Zenkō]] (617–700), a son of the last king of [[Baekje]], one of the [[Three Kingdoms of Korea]].<ref>{{cite web | url= http://www.asukanet.gr.jp/asukahome/ASUKA2/TAKAMATUTUKA/takamatutuka.html | work = Asuka | title = Takamatuzuka | publisher = Asuka net | place = [[Japan | JP]]}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url= http://www.nara-np.co.jp/special/takamatu/vol_02d_01.html |title=特集 高松塚光源 |website= Nara-np | place = JP |access-date= 13 January 2022 | language = ja |archive-url= https://web.archive.org/web/20070907112805/http://www.nara-np.co.jp/special/takamatu/vol_02d_01.html |archive-date=7 September 2007 |url-status=dead}}</ref>
==Conservation== The [[Cultural Affairs Agency]] of Japan is{{As of? |date=February 2024}} considering taking apart the stone chamber and reassembling it elsewhere to prevent further deterioration to its wall paintings. A painting called ''Asuka Bijin'', or "beautiful women", is one of the [[mural]]s in the tomb facing deterioration. The unusual preservation method is being considered because the tomb's current situation makes it impossible to prevent further damage and stop the spread of [[Mold (fungus)|mold]].
Unlike the [[Kitora Tomb]], in Asuka removing pieces of the Takamatsuzuka wall [[plaster]] and reinforcing them for conservation appears difficult because the plaster has numerous tiny cracks.{{Citation needed|date=June 2022}}
==Comparison== In 2012, similar mural was found in a tomb in [[Mongolia]]. The round mound, thought to be built by [[Göktürks]] in the 7th century, was excavated by Mongolian and Kazakh researchers. The mural depicts Azure Dragon and White Tiger with a procession of Chinese and [[Sogdians|Sogdian]], and other Caucasoid traders.

[[File:高松塚古墳.jpg|thumb|Takamatsuzuka Tomb]] The {{nihongo|'''Takamatsuzuka Tomb'''|高松塚古墳|''Takamatsuzuka Kofun''}} is an ancient {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular tomb|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} in [[Asuka, Nara|Asuka village]], [[Nara Prefecture]], [[Japan]].
==History== The tomb is thought to have been built at some time between the end of the 7th century and the beginning of the 8th century. It was accidentally discovered by a local farmer in the 1960s. [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 3]][[Category:qq 3]][[en:Takamatsuzuka Tomb]][[Category:kk]] ==Description== [[File:Takamat1.jpg|thumb|A mural of women on the western wall of the tomb]] The mound of the tomb was built of alternating layers of [[clay]] and [[sand]]. It is about {{convert |16|m}} in diameter and {{convert|5|m}} high. Excavation yielded a burial chamber with painted [[fresco]] wall paintings of [[courtier]]s in [[Goguryeo]]-style garb. The paintings are in full color with red, blue, gold, and silver foil representing four male followers and four serving maidens together with the [[Azure Dragon (Chinese constellation) |Azure Dragon]], [[Black Tortoise (Chinese constellation)|Black Tortoise]], [[White Tiger (Chinese constellation)|White Tiger]], and [[Vermilion Bird (Chinese constellation)|Vermilion Bird]] groups of stars. The paintings are [[Cultural Properties of Japan|designated]] a [[National Treasures of Japan|National Treasure]].
For whom the tomb was built is unknown, but the decorations suggest it is for a member of the Japanese royal family or a high-ranking nobleman. Candidates include:
# [[Prince Osakabe]] (d. 705), a son of [[Emperor Tenmu]] # [[Prince Yuge]] (d. 699), also a son of Emperor Tenmu # [[Prince Takechi]] ({{circa|654}} – 696), also a son of Emperor Temmu, general of [[Jinshin War]], [[Daijō Daijin]] # Isonokami Ason Maro (640–717), a descendant of [[Mononobe clan]] and in charge of [[Fujiwara-kyō]] after the capital was moved to [[Heijō-kyō]] # [[Kudara no Konikishi|Kudara no Konikishi Zenkō]] (617–700), a son of the last king of [[Baekje]], one of the [[Three Kingdoms of Korea]].<ref>{{cite web | url= http://www.asukanet.gr.jp/asukahome/ASUKA2/TAKAMATUTUKA/takamatutuka.html | work = Asuka | title = Takamatuzuka | publisher = Asuka net | place = [[Japan | JP]]}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url= http://www.nara-np.co.jp/special/takamatu/vol_02d_01.html |title=特集 高松塚光源 |website= Nara-np | place = JP |access-date= 13 January 2022 | language = ja |archive-url= https://web.archive.org/web/20070907112805/http://www.nara-np.co.jp/special/takamatu/vol_02d_01.html |archive-date=7 September 2007 |url-status=dead}}</ref>
==Conservation== The [[Cultural Affairs Agency]] of Japan is{{As of? |date=February 2024}} considering taking apart the stone chamber and reassembling it elsewhere to prevent further deterioration to its wall paintings. A painting called ''Asuka Bijin'', or "beautiful women", is one of the [[mural]]s in the tomb facing deterioration. The unusual preservation method is being considered because the tomb's current situation makes it impossible to prevent further damage and stop the spread of [[Mold (fungus)|mold]].
Unlike the [[Kitora Tomb]], in Asuka removing pieces of the Takamatsuzuka wall [[plaster]] and reinforcing them for conservation appears difficult because the plaster has numerous tiny cracks.{{Citation needed|date=June 2022}}
==Comparison== In 2012, similar mural was found in a tomb in [[Mongolia]]. The round mound, thought to be built by [[Göktürks]] in the 7th century, was excavated by Mongolian and Kazakh researchers. The mural depicts Azure Dragon and White Tiger with a procession of Chinese and [[Sogdians|Sogdian]], and other Caucasoid traders.

[[Category:kk]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[sn:Takamatsuzuka Tomb]][[Category:New]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Takamatsuzuka Tomb]][[Category:11]] [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]]

[[File:高松塚古墳.jpg|thumb|Takamatsuzuka Tomb]] The {{nihongo|'''Takamatsuzuka Tomb'''|高松塚古墳|''Takamatsuzuka Kofun''}} is an ancient {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|lt=circular tomb|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} in [[Asuka, Nara|Asuka village]], [[Nara Prefecture]], [[Japan]].
==History== The tomb is thought to have been built at some time between the end of the 7th century and the beginning of the 8th century. It was accidentally discovered by a local farmer in the 1960s. [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 3]][[Category:11 3]][[en:Takamatsuzuka Tomb]][[Category:kk]] ==Description== [[File:Takamat1.jpg|thumb|A mural of women on the western wall of the tomb]] The mound of the tomb was built of alternating layers of [[clay]] and [[sand]]. It is about {{convert |16|m}} in diameter and {{convert|5|m}} high. Excavation yielded a burial chamber with painted [[fresco]] wall paintings of [[courtier]]s in [[Goguryeo]]-style garb. The paintings are in full color with red, blue, gold, and silver foil representing four male followers and four serving maidens together with the [[Azure Dragon (Chinese constellation) |Azure Dragon]], [[Black Tortoise (Chinese constellation)|Black Tortoise]], [[White Tiger (Chinese constellation)|White Tiger]], and [[Vermilion Bird (Chinese constellation)|Vermilion Bird]] groups of stars. The paintings are [[Cultural Properties of Japan|designated]] a [[National Treasures of Japan|National Treasure]].
For whom the tomb was built is unknown, but the decorations suggest it is for a member of the Japanese royal family or a high-ranking nobleman. Candidates include:
# [[Prince Osakabe]] (d. 705), a son of [[Emperor Tenmu]] # [[Prince Yuge]] (d. 699), also a son of Emperor Tenmu # [[Prince Takechi]] ({{circa|654}} – 696), also a son of Emperor Temmu, general of [[Jinshin War]], [[Daijō Daijin]] # Isonokami Ason Maro (640–717), a descendant of [[Mononobe clan]] and in charge of [[Fujiwara-kyō]] after the capital was moved to [[Heijō-kyō]] # [[Kudara no Konikishi|Kudara no Konikishi Zenkō]] (617–700), a son of the last king of [[Baekje]], one of the [[Three Kingdoms of Korea]].<ref>{{cite web | url= http://www.asukanet.gr.jp/asukahome/ASUKA2/TAKAMATUTUKA/takamatutuka.html | work = Asuka | title = Takamatuzuka | publisher = Asuka net | place = [[Japan | JP]]}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url= http://www.nara-np.co.jp/special/takamatu/vol_02d_01.html |title=特集 高松塚光源 |website= Nara-np | place = JP |access-date= 13 January 2022 | language = ja |archive-url= https://web.archive.org/web/20070907112805/http://www.nara-np.co.jp/special/takamatu/vol_02d_01.html |archive-date=7 September 2007 |url-status=dead}}</ref>
==Conservation== The [[Cultural Affairs Agency]] of Japan is{{As of? |date=February 2024}} considering taking apart the stone chamber and reassembling it elsewhere to prevent further deterioration to its wall paintings. A painting called ''Asuka Bijin'', or "beautiful women", is one of the [[mural]]s in the tomb facing deterioration. The unusual preservation method is being considered because the tomb's current situation makes it impossible to prevent further damage and stop the spread of [[Mold (fungus)|mold]].
Unlike the [[Kitora Tomb]], in Asuka removing pieces of the Takamatsuzuka wall [[plaster]] and reinforcing them for conservation appears difficult because the plaster has numerous tiny cracks.{{Citation needed|date=June 2022}}
==Comparison== In 2012, similar mural was found in a tomb in [[Mongolia]]. The round mound, thought to be built by [[Göktürks]] in the 7th century, was excavated by Mongolian and Kazakh researchers. The mural depicts Azure Dragon and White Tiger with a procession of Chinese and [[Sogdians|Sogdian]], and other Caucasoid traders.
== Gallery == <gallery> File:Takamatsuzuka Asuka Bijin.jpg|A detail of the ''Asuka Bijin'' mural in the tomb File:高松塚古墳壁画 (複製).JPG|A reproduction of the murals at [[Kansai University]] Museum File:Asuka Byako.JPEG|A painting of a [[White Tiger (mythology)|''byakko'']] in the tomb </gallery>
==See also== *[[Detachment of wall paintings]] *[[List of National Treasures of Japan (paintings)]] *[[List of Special Places of Scenic Beauty, Special Historic Sites and Special Natural Monuments]] *[[Asuka-Fujiwara]]
==References== {{Reflist}}
==External links== {{commons Category|Takamatsuzuka_Kofun|Takamatsuzuka Tomb}} *[https://web.archive.org/web/20110305022936/http://www.pref.nara.jp/nara/kaido/eg/ko_nara/spot/k05.htm Takamatsuzuka ancient Kofun] *[http://www.japantimes.co.jp/weekly/news/nn2005/nn20050702a5.htm The Japan Times Weekly Online - Takamatsuzuka tomb to be moved] *[https://web.archive.org/web/20060503114824/http://gias.snu.ac.kr/wthong/publication/paekche/eng/hi3-5.pdf Paekche Culture in Asuka Japan] * [http://www.nabunken.go.jp/asuka/index.html Asuka Historical Museum]{{in lang|ja}}

[[File:高松塚古墳.jpg|thumb|Takamatsuzuka Tomb]] The {{nihongo|'''Takamatsuzuka Tomb'''|高松塚古墳|''Takamatsuzuka Kofun''}} is an ancient {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|lt=circular tomb|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} in [[Asuka, Nara|Asuka village]], [[Nara Prefecture]], [[Japan]].
==History== The tomb is thought to have been built at some time between the end of the 7th century and the beginning of the 8th century. It was accidentally discovered by a local farmer in the 1960s. [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 3]][[Category:11 3]][[en:Takamatsuzuka Tomb]][[Category:kk]] ==Description== [[File:Takamat1.jpg|thumb|A mural of women on the western wall of the tomb]] The mound of the tomb was built of alternating layers of [[clay]] and [[sand]]. It is about {{convert |16|m}} in diameter and {{convert|5|m}} high. Excavation yielded a burial chamber with painted [[fresco]] wall paintings of [[courtier]]s in [[Goguryeo]]-style garb. The paintings are in full color with red, blue, gold, and silver foil representing four male followers and four serving maidens together with the [[Azure Dragon (Chinese constellation) |Azure Dragon]], [[Black Tortoise (Chinese constellation)|Black Tortoise]], [[White Tiger (Chinese constellation)|White Tiger]], and [[Vermilion Bird (Chinese constellation)|Vermilion Bird]] groups of stars. The paintings are [[Cultural Properties of Japan|designated]] a [[National Treasures of Japan|National Treasure]].
For whom the tomb was built is unknown, but the decorations suggest it is for a member of the Japanese royal family or a high-ranking nobleman. Candidates include:
# [[Prince Osakabe]] (d. 705), a son of [[Emperor Tenmu]] # [[Prince Yuge]] (d. 699), also a son of Emperor Tenmu # [[Prince Takechi]] ({{circa|654}} – 696), also a son of Emperor Temmu, general of [[Jinshin War]], [[Daijō Daijin]] # Isonokami Ason Maro (640–717), a descendant of [[Mononobe clan]] and in charge of [[Fujiwara-kyō]] after the capital was moved to [[Heijō-kyō]] # [[Kudara no Konikishi|Kudara no Konikishi Zenkō]] (617–700), a son of the last king of [[Baekje]], one of the [[Three Kingdoms of Korea]].<ref>{{cite web | url= http://www.asukanet.gr.jp/asukahome/ASUKA2/TAKAMATUTUKA/takamatutuka.html | work = Asuka | title = Takamatuzuka | publisher = Asuka net | place = [[Japan | JP]]}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url= http://www.nara-np.co.jp/special/takamatu/vol_02d_01.html |title=特集 高松塚光源 |website= Nara-np | place = JP |access-date= 13 January 2022 | language = ja |archive-url= https://web.archive.org/web/20070907112805/http://www.nara-np.co.jp/special/takamatu/vol_02d_01.html |archive-date=7 September 2007 |url-status=dead}}</ref>
==Conservation== The [[Cultural Affairs Agency]] of Japan is{{As of? |date=February 2024}} considering taking apart the stone chamber and reassembling it elsewhere to prevent further deterioration to its wall paintings. A painting called ''Asuka Bijin'', or "beautiful women", is one of the [[mural]]s in the tomb facing deterioration. The unusual preservation method is being considered because the tomb's current situation makes it impossible to prevent further damage and stop the spread of [[Mold (fungus)|mold]].
Unlike the [[Kitora Tomb]], in Asuka removing pieces of the Takamatsuzuka wall [[plaster]] and reinforcing them for conservation appears difficult because the plaster has numerous tiny cracks.{{Citation needed|date=June 2022}}
==Comparison== In 2012, similar mural was found in a tomb in [[Mongolia]]. The round mound, thought to be built by [[Göktürks]] in the 7th century, was excavated by Mongolian and Kazakh researchers. The mural depicts Azure Dragon and White Tiger with a procession of Chinese and [[Sogdians|Sogdian]], and other Caucasoid traders.
== Gallery == <gallery> File:Takamatsuzuka Asuka Bijin.jpg|A detail of the ''Asuka Bijin'' mural in the tomb File:高松塚古墳壁画 (複製).JPG|A reproduction of the murals at [[Kansai University]] Museum File:Asuka Byako.JPEG|A painting of a [[White Tiger (mythology)|''byakko'']] in the tomb </gallery>
==See also== *[[Detachment of wall paintings]] *[[List of National Treasures of Japan (paintings)]] *[[List of Special Places of Scenic Beauty, Special Historic Sites and Special Natural Monuments]] *[[Asuka-Fujiwara]]

[[File:高松塚古墳.jpg|thumb|Takamatsuzuka Tomb]] The {{nihongo|'''Takamatsuzuka Tomb'''|高松塚古墳|''Takamatsuzuka Kofun''}} is an ancient {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|lt=circular tomb|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} in [[Asuka, Nara|Asuka village]], [[Nara Prefecture]], [[Japan]].
==History== The tomb is thought to have been built at some time between the end of the 7th century and the beginning of the 8th century. It was accidentally discovered by a local farmer in the 1960s. [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 3]][[en:Takamatsuzuka Tomb]][[Category:kk]] ==Description== [[File:Takamat1.jpg|thumb|A mural of women on the western wall of the tomb]] The mound of the tomb was built of alternating layers of [[clay]] and [[sand]]. It is about {{convert |16|m}} in diameter and {{convert|5|m}} high. Excavation yielded a burial chamber with painted [[fresco]] wall paintings of [[courtier]]s in [[Goguryeo]]-style garb. The paintings are in full color with red, blue, gold, and silver foil representing four male followers and four serving maidens together with the [[Azure Dragon (Chinese constellation) |Azure Dragon]], [[Black Tortoise (Chinese constellation)|Black Tortoise]], [[White Tiger (Chinese constellation)|White Tiger]], and [[Vermilion Bird (Chinese constellation)|Vermilion Bird]] groups of stars. The paintings are [[Cultural Properties of Japan|designated]] a [[National Treasures of Japan|National Treasure]].
For whom the tomb was built is unknown, but the decorations suggest it is for a member of the Japanese royal family or a high-ranking nobleman. Candidates include:
# [[Prince Osakabe]] (d. 705), a son of [[Emperor Tenmu]] # [[Prince Yuge]] (d. 699), also a son of Emperor Tenmu # [[Prince Takechi]] ({{circa|654}} – 696), also a son of Emperor Temmu, general of [[Jinshin War]], [[Daijō Daijin]] # Isonokami Ason Maro (640–717), a descendant of [[Mononobe clan]] and in charge of [[Fujiwara-kyō]] after the capital was moved to [[Heijō-kyō]] # [[Kudara no Konikishi|Kudara no Konikishi Zenkō]] (617–700), a son of the last king of [[Baekje]], one of the [[Three Kingdoms of Korea]].<ref>{{cite web | url= http://www.asukanet.gr.jp/asukahome/ASUKA2/TAKAMATUTUKA/takamatutuka.html | work = Asuka | title = Takamatuzuka | publisher = Asuka net | place = [[Japan | JP]]}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url= http://www.nara-np.co.jp/special/takamatu/vol_02d_01.html |title=特集 高松塚光源 |website= Nara-np | place = JP |access-date= 13 January 2022 | language = ja |archive-url= https://web.archive.org/web/20070907112805/http://www.nara-np.co.jp/special/takamatu/vol_02d_01.html |archive-date=7 September 2007 |url-status=dead}}</ref>
==Conservation== The [[Cultural Affairs Agency]] of Japan is{{As of? |date=February 2024}} considering taking apart the stone chamber and reassembling it elsewhere to prevent further deterioration to its wall paintings. A painting called ''Asuka Bijin'', or "beautiful women", is one of the [[mural]]s in the tomb facing deterioration. The unusual preservation method is being considered because the tomb's current situation makes it impossible to prevent further damage and stop the spread of [[Mold (fungus)|mold]].
Unlike the [[Kitora Tomb]], in Asuka removing pieces of the Takamatsuzuka wall [[plaster]] and reinforcing them for conservation appears difficult because the plaster has numerous tiny cracks.{{Citation needed|date=June 2022}}
==Comparison== In 2012, similar mural was found in a tomb in [[Mongolia]]. The round mound, thought to be built by [[Göktürks]] in the 7th century, was excavated by Mongolian and Kazakh researchers. The mural depicts Azure Dragon and White Tiger with a procession of Chinese and [[Sogdians|Sogdian]], and other Caucasoid traders.
== Gallery == <gallery> File:Takamatsuzuka Asuka Bijin.jpg|A detail of the ''Asuka Bijin'' mural in the tomb File:高松塚古墳壁画 (複製).JPG|A reproduction of the murals at [[Kansai University]] Museum File:Asuka Byako.JPEG|A painting of a [[White Tiger (mythology)|''byakko'']] in the tomb </gallery>
==See also== *[[Detachment of wall paintings]] *[[List of National Treasures of Japan (paintings)]] *[[List of Special Places of Scenic Beauty, Special Historic Sites and Special Natural Monuments]] *[[Asuka-Fujiwara]]

==Conservation== The [[Cultural Affairs Agency]] of Japan is{{As of? |date=February 2024}} considering taking apart the stone chamber and reassembling it elsewhere to prevent further deterioration to its wall paintings. A painting called ''Asuka Bijin'', or "beautiful women", is one of the [[mural]]s in the tomb facing deterioration. The unusual preservation method is being considered because the tomb's current situation makes it impossible to prevent further damage and stop the spread of [[Mold (fungus)|mold]].
Unlike the [[Kitora Tomb]], in Asuka removing pieces of the Takamatsuzuka wall [[plaster]] and reinforcing them for conservation appears difficult because the plaster has numerous tiny cracks.{{Citation needed|date=June 2022}}
==Comparison== In 2012, similar mural was found in a tomb in [[Mongolia]]. The round mound, thought to be built by [[Göktürks]] in the 7th century, was excavated by Mongolian and Kazakh researchers. The mural depicts Azure Dragon and White Tiger with a procession of Chinese and [[Sogdians|Sogdian]], and other Caucasoid traders.
== Gallery == <gallery> File:Takamatsuzuka Asuka Bijin.jpg|A detail of the ''Asuka Bijin'' mural in the tomb File:高松塚古墳壁画 (複製).JPG|A reproduction of the murals at [[Kansai University]] Museum File:Asuka Byako.JPEG|A painting of a [[White Tiger (mythology)|''byakko'']] in the tomb </gallery>
==See also== *[[Detachment of wall paintings]] *[[List of National Treasures of Japan (paintings)]] *[[List of Special Places of Scenic Beauty, Special Historic Sites and Special Natural Monuments]] *[[Asuka-Fujiwara]]

revision.text:
[[File:高松塚古墳.jpg|thumb|Takamatsuzuka Tomb]] The {{nihongo|'''Takamatsuzuka Tomb'''|高松塚古墳|''Takamatsuzuka Kofun''}} is an ancient {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|lt=circular tomb|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} in [[Asuka, Nara|Asuka village]], [[Nara Prefecture]], [[Japan]].
==History== The tomb is thought to have been built at some time between the end of the 7th century and the beginning of the 8th century. It was accidentally discovered by a local farmer in the 1960s. [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 3]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takamatsuzuka Tomb]][[Category:qq]] [[en:Takamatsuzuka Tomb]][[Category:kk]] ==Description== [[File:Takamat1.jpg|thumb|A mural of women on the western wall of the tomb]] The mound of the tomb was built of alternating layers of [[clay]] and [[sand]]. It is about {{convert |16|m}} in diameter and {{convert|5|m}} high. Excavation yielded a burial chamber with painted [[fresco]] wall paintings of [[courtier]]s in [[Goguryeo]]-style garb. The paintings are in full color with red, blue, gold, and silver foil representing four male followers and four serving maidens together with the [[Azure Dragon (Chinese constellation) |Azure Dragon]], [[Black Tortoise (Chinese constellation)|Black Tortoise]], [[White Tiger (Chinese constellation)|White Tiger]], and [[Vermilion Bird (Chinese constellation)|Vermilion Bird]] groups of stars. The paintings are [[Cultural Properties of Japan|designated]] a [[National Treasures of Japan|National Treasure]].
For whom the tomb was built is unknown, but the decorations suggest it is for a member of the Japanese royal family or a high-ranking nobleman. Candidates include:
# [[Prince Osakabe]] (d. 705), a son of [[Emperor Tenmu]] # [[Prince Yuge]] (d. 699), also a son of Emperor Tenmu # [[Prince Takechi]] ({{circa|654}} – 696), also a son of Emperor Temmu, general of [[Jinshin War]], [[Daijō Daijin]] # Isonokami Ason Maro (640–717), a descendant of [[Mononobe clan]] and in charge of [[Fujiwara-kyō]] after the capital was moved to [[Heijō-kyō]] # [[Kudara no Konikishi|Kudara no Konikishi Zenkō]] (617–700), a son of the last king of [[Baekje]], one of the [[Three Kingdoms of Korea]].<ref>{{cite web | url= http://www.asukanet.gr.jp/asukahome/ASUKA2/TAKAMATUTUKA/takamatutuka.html | work = Asuka | title = Takamatuzuka | publisher = Asuka net | place = [[Japan | JP]]}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url= http://www.nara-np.co.jp/special/takamatu/vol_02d_01.html |title=特集 高松塚光源 |website= Nara-np | place = JP |access-date= 13 January 2022 | language = ja |archive-url= https://web.archive.org/web/20070907112805/http://www.nara-np.co.jp/special/takamatu/vol_02d_01.html |archive-date=7 September 2007 |url-status=dead}}</ref>
==Conservation== The [[Cultural Affairs Agency]] of Japan is{{As of? |date=February 2024}} considering taking apart the stone chamber and reassembling it elsewhere to prevent further deterioration to its wall paintings. A painting called ''Asuka Bijin'', or "beautiful women", is one of the [[mural]]s in the tomb facing deterioration. The unusual preservation method is being considered because the tomb's current situation makes it impossible to prevent further damage and stop the spread of [[Mold (fungus)|mold]].
Unlike the [[Kitora Tomb]], in Asuka removing pieces of the Takamatsuzuka wall [[plaster]] and reinforcing them for conservation appears difficult because the plaster has numerous tiny cracks.{{Citation needed|date=June 2022}}
==Comparison== In 2012, similar mural was found in a tomb in [[Mongolia]]. The round mound, thought to be built by [[Göktürks]] in the 7th century, was excavated by Mongolian and Kazakh researchers. The mural depicts Azure Dragon and White Tiger with a procession of Chinese and [[Sogdians|Sogdian]], and other Caucasoid traders.
== Gallery == <gallery> File:Takamatsuzuka Asuka Bijin.jpg|A detail of the ''Asuka Bijin'' mural in the tomb File:高松塚古墳壁画 (複製).JPG|A reproduction of the murals at [[Kansai University]] Museum File:Asuka Byako.JPEG|A painting of a [[White Tiger (mythology)|''byakko'']] in the tomb </gallery>
==See also== *[[Detachment of wall paintings]] *[[List of National Treasures of Japan (paintings)]] *[[List of Special Places of Scenic Beauty, Special Historic Sites and Special Natural Monuments]] *[[Asuka-Fujiwara]]
==References== {{Reflist}}
==External links== {{commons Category|Takamatsuzuka_Kofun|Takamatsuzuka Tomb}} *[https://web.archive.org/web/20110305022936/http://www.pref.nara.jp/nara/kaido/eg/ko_nara/spot/k05.htm Takamatsuzuka ancient Kofun] *[http://www.japantimes.co.jp/weekly/news/nn2005/nn20050702a5.htm The Japan Times Weekly Online - Takamatsuzuka tomb to be moved] *[https://web.archive.org/web/20060503114824/http://gias.snu.ac.kr/wthong/publication/paekche/eng/hi3-5.pdf Paekche Culture in Asuka Japan] * [http://www.nabunken.go.jp/asuka/index.html Asuka Historical Museum]{{in lang|ja}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Takamatsuzuka Kofun]][[de:Takamatsuzuka Kofun]][[en:Takamatsuzuka Tomb]][[fa:مقبره تاکاماتسوکا]][[fr:Kofun de Takamatsuzuka]][[it:Takamatsuzuka]][[ja:高松塚古墳]][[ko:다카마쓰 고분]][[ml:തക്കമത്സുക ശവകുടീരം]][[pl:Takamatsuzuka]][[zh:高松塚古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|image=File:Takamatsuzuka 2014.JPG|location={{ill|Asuka|qid=Q752397}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.46222222|N|135.80647222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles={{ill|circular kofun|qid=Q11394747}}|part_of={{ill|Asuka Historical National Government Park|qid=Q4811855}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q1039675]] Wikidata name: Takamatsuzuka Tomb [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:44, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:高松塚古墳.jpg|thumb|Takamatsuzuka Tomb]] The {{nihongo|'''Takamatsuzuka Tomb'''|高松塚古墳|''Takamatsuzuka Kofun''}} is an ancient {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular tomb|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} in [[:en:Asuka, Nara|Asuka village]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]].
==History== The tomb is thought to have been built at some time between the end of the 7th century and the beginning of the 8th century. It was accidentally discovered by a local farmer in the 1960s. ==Description== [[File:Takamat1.jpg|thumb|A mural of women on the western wall of the tomb]] The mound of the tomb was built of alternating layers of [[:en:Clay|Clay]] and [[:en:sand|sand]]. It is about {{convert |16|m}} in diameter and {{convert|5|m}} high. Excavation yielded a burial chamber with painted [[:en:fresco|fresco]] wall paintings of [[:en:Courtier|Courtier]]s in [[:en:Goguryeo|Goguryeo]]-style garb. The paintings are in full color with red, blue, gold, and silver foil representing four male followers and four serving maidens together with the [[:en:Azure Dragon (Chinese constellation) |Azure Dragon]], [[:en:Black Tortoise (Chinese constellation)|Black Tortoise]], [[:en:White Tiger (Chinese constellation)|White Tiger]], and [[:en:Vermilion Bird (Chinese constellation)|Vermilion Bird]] groups of stars. The paintings are [[:en:Cultural Property (Japan)|designated]] a [[:en:National Treasure (Japan)|National Treasure]].
For whom the tomb was built is unknown, but the decorations suggest it is for a member of the Japanese royal family or a high-Ranking nobleman. Candidates include:
# [[:en:Prince Osakabe|Prince Osakabe]] (d. 705), a son of [[:en:Emperor Tenmu|Emperor Tenmu]] # [[:en:Prince Yuge|Prince Yuge]] (d. 699), also a son of Emperor Tenmu # [[:en:Prince Takechi|Prince Takechi]] ({{circa|654}} – 696), also a son of Emperor Temmu, general of [[:en:Jinshin War|Jinshin War]], [[:en:Daijō Daijin|Daijō Daijin]] # Isonokami Ason Maro (640–717), a descendant of [[Mononobe clan]] and in charge of [[:en:Fujiwara-kyō|Fujiwara-kyō]] after the capital was moved to [[Heijō-kyō]] # [[:en:Kudara no Konikishi|Kudara no Konikishi Zenkō]] (617–700), a son of the last king of [[:en:Baekje|Baekje]], one of the [[:en:Three Kingdoms of Korea|Three Kingdoms of Korea]].<ref>{{cite web | url= http://www.asukanet.gr.jp/asukahome/ASUKA2/TAKAMATUTUKA/takamatutuka.html | work = Asuka | title = Takamatuzuka | publisher = Asuka net | place = [[:en:Japan | JP]]}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url= http://www.nara-np.co.jp/special/takamatu/vol_02d_01.html |title=特集 高松塚光源 |website= Nara-np | place = JP |access-date= 13 January 2022 | language = ja |archive-url= https://web.archive.org/web/20070907112805/http://www.nara-np.co.jp/special/takamatu/vol_02d_01.html |archive-date=7 September 2007 |url-status=dead}}</ref>
==Conservation== The [[:en:Cultural Affairs Agency|Cultural Affairs Agency]] of Japan is{{As of? |date=February 2024}} considering taking apart the stone chamber and reassembling it elsewhere to prevent further deterioration to its wall paintings. A painting called ''Asuka Bijin'', or "beautiful women", is one of the [[:en:mural|mural]]s in the tomb facing deterioration. The unusual preservation method is being considered because the tomb's current situation makes it impossible to prevent further damage and stop the spread of [[:en:Mold (fungus)|mold]].
Unlike the [[Kitora Tomb]], in Asuka removing pieces of the Takamatsuzuka wall [[:en:Plaster|Plaster]] and reinforcing them for conservation appears difficult because the plaster has numerous tiny cracks.{{Citation needed|date=June 2022}}
==Comparison== In 2012, similar mural was found in a tomb in [[:en:Mongolia|Mongolia]]. The round mound, thought to be built by [[:en:Göktürks|Göktürks]] in the 7th century, was excavated by Mongolian and Kazakh researchers. The mural depicts Azure Dragon and White Tiger with a procession of Chinese and [[:en:Sogdians|Sogdian]], and other Caucasoid traders.
== Gallery == <gallery> File:Takamatsuzuka Asuka Bijin.jpg|A detail of the ''Asuka Bijin'' mural in the tomb File:高松塚古墳壁画 (複製).JPG|A reproduction of the murals at [[:en:Kansai University|Kansai University]] Museum File:Asuka Byako.JPEG|A painting of a [[:en:White Tiger (mythology)|''byakko'']] in the tomb </gallery>
==See also== *[[:en:Detachment of wall paintings|Detachment of wall paintings]] *[[:en:en:List of National Treasures of Japan (paintings)|en:List of National Treasures of Japan (paintings)]] *[[:en:List of Special Places of Scenic Beauty, Special Historic Sites and Special Natural Monuments|List of Special Places of Scenic Beauty, Special Historic Sites and Special Natural Monuments]] *[[:en:Asuka-Fujiwara|Asuka-Fujiwara]]

It was accidentally discovered by a local farmer in the 1960s. ==Description== [[File:Takamat1.jpg|thumb|A mural of women on the western wall of the tomb]] The mound of the tomb was built of alternating layers of [[:en:Clay|Clay]] and [[:en:sand|sand]]. It is about {{convert |16|m}} in diameter and {{convert|5|m}} high. Excavation yielded a burial chamber with painted [[:en:fresco|fresco]] wall paintings of [[:en:Courtier|Courtier]]s in [[:en:Goguryeo|Goguryeo]]-style garb. The paintings are in full color with red, blue, gold, and silver foil representing four male followers and four serving maidens together with the [[:en:Azure Dragon (Chinese constellation) |Azure Dragon]], [[:en:Black Tortoise (Chinese constellation)|Black Tortoise]], [[:en:White Tiger (Chinese constellation)|White Tiger]], and [[:en:Vermilion Bird (Chinese constellation)|Vermilion Bird]] groups of stars. The paintings are [[:en:Cultural Property (Japan)|designated]] a [[:en:National Treasure (Japan)|National Treasure]].
For whom the tomb was built is unknown, but the decorations suggest it is for a member of the Japanese royal family or a high-Ranking nobleman. Candidates include:
# [[:en:Prince Osakabe|Prince Osakabe]] (d. 705), a son of [[:en:Emperor Tenmu|Emperor Tenmu]] # [[:en:Prince Yuge|Prince Yuge]] (d. 699), also a son of Emperor Tenmu # [[:en:Prince Takechi|Prince Takechi]] ({{circa|654}} – 696), also a son of Emperor Temmu, general of [[:en:Jinshin War|Jinshin War]], [[:en:Daijō Daijin|Daijō Daijin]] # Isonokami Ason Maro (640–717), a descendant of [[Mononobe clan]] and in charge of [[:en:Fujiwara-kyō|Fujiwara-kyō]] after the capital was moved to [[Heijō-kyō]] # [[:en:Kudara no Konikishi|Kudara no Konikishi Zenkō]] (617–700), a son of the last king of [[:en:Baekje|Baekje]], one of the [[:en:Three Kingdoms of Korea|Three Kingdoms of Korea]].<ref>{{cite web | url= http://www.asukanet.gr.jp/asukahome/ASUKA2/TAKAMATUTUKA/takamatutuka.html | work = Asuka | title = Takamatuzuka | publisher = Asuka net | place = [[:en:Japan | JP]]}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url= http://www.nara-np.co.jp/special/takamatu/vol_02d_01.html |title=特集 高松塚光源 |website= Nara-np | place = JP |access-date= 13 January 2022 | language = ja |archive-url= https://web.archive.org/web/20070907112805/http://www.nara-np.co.jp/special/takamatu/vol_02d_01.html |archive-date=7 September 2007 |url-status=dead}}</ref>
==Conservation== The [[:en:Cultural Affairs Agency|Cultural Affairs Agency]] of Japan is{{As of? |date=February 2024}} considering taking apart the stone chamber and reassembling it elsewhere to prevent further deterioration to its wall paintings. A painting called ''Asuka Bijin'', or "beautiful women", is one of the [[:en:mural|mural]]s in the tomb facing deterioration. The unusual preservation method is being considered because the tomb's current situation makes it impossible to prevent further damage and stop the spread of [[:en:Mold (fungus)|mold]].
Unlike the [[Kitora Tomb]], in Asuka removing pieces of the Takamatsuzuka wall [[:en:Plaster|Plaster]] and reinforcing them for conservation appears difficult because the plaster has numerous tiny cracks.{{Citation needed|date=June 2022}}
==Comparison== In 2012, similar mural was found in a tomb in [[:en:Mongolia|Mongolia]]. The round mound, thought to be built by [[:en:Göktürks|Göktürks]] in the 7th century, was excavated by Mongolian and Kazakh researchers. The mural depicts Azure Dragon and White Tiger with a procession of Chinese and [[:en:Sogdians|Sogdian]], and other Caucasoid traders.
== Gallery == <gallery> File:Takamatsuzuka Asuka Bijin.jpg|A detail of the ''Asuka Bijin'' mural in the tomb File:高松塚古墳壁画 (複製).JPG|A reproduction of the murals at [[:en:Kansai University|Kansai University]] Museum File:Asuka Byako.JPEG|A painting of a [[:en:White Tiger (mythology)|''byakko'']] in the tomb </gallery>

The paintings are [[:en:Cultural Property (Japan)|designated]] a [[:en:National Treasure (Japan)|National Treasure]].
For whom the tomb was built is unknown, but the decorations suggest it is for a member of the Japanese royal family or a high-Ranking nobleman. Candidates include:
# [[:en:Prince Osakabe|Prince Osakabe]] (d. 705), a son of [[:en:Emperor Tenmu|Emperor Tenmu]] # [[:en:Prince Yuge|Prince Yuge]] (d. 699), also a son of Emperor Tenmu # [[:en:Prince Takechi|Prince Takechi]] ({{circa|654}} – 696), also a son of Emperor Temmu, general of [[:en:Jinshin War|Jinshin War]], [[:en:Daijō Daijin|Daijō Daijin]] # Isonokami Ason Maro (640–717), a descendant of [[Mononobe clan]] and in charge of [[:en:Fujiwara-kyō|Fujiwara-kyō]] after the capital was moved to [[Heijō-kyō]] # [[:en:Kudara no Konikishi|Kudara no Konikishi Zenkō]] (617–700), a son of the last king of [[:en:Baekje|Baekje]], one of the [[:en:Three Kingdoms of Korea|Three Kingdoms of Korea]].<ref>{{cite web | url= http://www.asukanet.gr.jp/asukahome/ASUKA2/TAKAMATUTUKA/takamatutuka.html | work = Asuka | title = Takamatuzuka | publisher = Asuka net | place = [[:en:Japan | JP]]}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url= http://www.nara-np.co.jp/special/takamatu/vol_02d_01.html |title=特集 高松塚光源 |website= Nara-np | place = JP |access-date= 13 January 2022 | language = ja |archive-url= https://web.archive.org/web/20070907112805/http://www.nara-np.co.jp/special/takamatu/vol_02d_01.html |archive-date=7 September 2007 |url-status=dead}}</ref>
==Conservation== The [[:en:Cultural Affairs Agency|Cultural Affairs Agency]] of Japan is{{As of? |date=February 2024}} considering taking apart the stone chamber and reassembling it elsewhere to prevent further deterioration to its wall paintings. A painting called ''Asuka Bijin'', or "beautiful women", is one of the [[:en:mural|mural]]s in the tomb facing deterioration. The unusual preservation method is being considered because the tomb's current situation makes it impossible to prevent further damage and stop the spread of [[:en:Mold (fungus)|mold]].
Unlike the [[Kitora Tomb]], in Asuka removing pieces of the Takamatsuzuka wall [[:en:Plaster|Plaster]] and reinforcing them for conservation appears difficult because the plaster has numerous tiny cracks.{{Citation needed|date=June 2022}}
==Comparison== In 2012, similar mural was found in a tomb in [[:en:Mongolia|Mongolia]]. The round mound, thought to be built by [[:en:Göktürks|Göktürks]] in the 7th century, was excavated by Mongolian and Kazakh researchers. The mural depicts Azure Dragon and White Tiger with a procession of Chinese and [[:en:Sogdians|Sogdian]], and other Caucasoid traders.
== Gallery == <gallery> File:Takamatsuzuka Asuka Bijin.jpg|A detail of the ''Asuka Bijin'' mural in the tomb File:高松塚古墳壁画 (複製).JPG|A reproduction of the murals at [[:en:Kansai University|Kansai University]] Museum File:Asuka Byako.JPEG|A painting of a [[:en:White Tiger (mythology)|''byakko'']] in the tomb </gallery>
