<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Takamatsuzuka Kofun]][[de:Takamatsuzuka Kofun]][[en:Takamatsuzuka Tomb]][[fa:مقبره تاکاماتسوکا]][[fr:Kofun de Takamatsuzuka]][[it:Takamatsuzuka]][[ja:高松塚古墳]][[ko:다카마쓰 고분]][[ml:തക്കമത്സുക ശവകുടീരം]][[pl:Takamatsuzuka]][[zh:高松塚古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|image=File:Takamatsuzuka 2014.JPG|location={{ill|Asuka|WD=Q752397}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.46222222|N|135.80647222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles={{ill|circular kofun|WD=Q11394747}}|part_of={{ill|Asuka Historical National Government Park|WD=Q4811855}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q1039675]] Wikidata name: Takamatsuzuka Tomb [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:44, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:高松塚古墳.jpg|thumb|Takamatsuzuka Tomb]] The {{nihongo|'''Takamatsuzuka Tomb'''|高松塚古墳|''Takamatsuzuka Kofun''}} is an ancient {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular tomb|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} in [[:en:Asuka, Nara|Asuka village]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]].
==History== The tomb is thought to have been built at some time between the end of the 7th century and the beginning of the 8th century. It was accidentally discovered by a local farmer in the 1960s. ==Description== [[File:Takamat1.jpg|thumb|A mural of women on the western wall of the tomb]] The mound of the tomb was built of alternating layers of [[:en:Clay|Clay]] and [[:en:sand|sand]]. It is about {{convert |16|m}} in diameter and {{convert|5|m}} high. Excavation yielded a burial chamber with painted [[:en:fresco|fresco]] wall paintings of [[:en:Courtier|Courtier]]s in [[:en:Goguryeo|Goguryeo]]-style garb. The paintings are in full color with red, blue, gold, and silver foil representing four male followers and four serving maidens together with the [[:en:Azure Dragon (Chinese constellation) |Azure Dragon]], [[:en:Black Tortoise (Chinese constellation)|Black Tortoise]], [[:en:White Tiger (Chinese constellation)|White Tiger]], and [[:en:Vermilion Bird (Chinese constellation)|Vermilion Bird]] groups of stars. The paintings are [[:en:Cultural Property (Japan)|designated]] a [[:en:National Treasure (Japan)|National Treasure]].
For whom the tomb was built is unknown, but the decorations suggest it is for a member of the Japanese royal family or a high-Ranking nobleman. Candidates include:
# [[:en:Prince Osakabe|Prince Osakabe]] (d. 705), a son of [[:en:Emperor Tenmu|Emperor Tenmu]] # [[:en:Prince Yuge|Prince Yuge]] (d. 699), also a son of Emperor Tenmu # [[:en:Prince Takechi|Prince Takechi]] ({{circa|654}} – 696), also a son of Emperor Temmu, general of [[:en:Jinshin War|Jinshin War]], [[:en:Daijō Daijin|Daijō Daijin]] # Isonokami Ason Maro (640–717), a descendant of [[Mononobe clan]] and in charge of [[:en:Fujiwara-kyō|Fujiwara-kyō]] after the capital was moved to [[Heijō-kyō]] # [[:en:Kudara no Konikishi|Kudara no Konikishi Zenkō]] (617–700), a son of the last king of [[:en:Baekje|Baekje]], one of the [[:en:Three Kingdoms of Korea|Three Kingdoms of Korea]].<ref>{{cite web | url= http://www.asukanet.gr.jp/asukahome/ASUKA2/TAKAMATUTUKA/takamatutuka.html | work = Asuka | title = Takamatuzuka | publisher = Asuka net | place = [[:en:Japan | JP]]}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url= http://www.nara-np.co.jp/special/takamatu/vol_02d_01.html |title=特集 高松塚光源 |website= Nara-np | place = JP |access-date= 13 January 2022 | language = ja |archive-url= https://web.archive.org/web/20070907112805/http://www.nara-np.co.jp/special/takamatu/vol_02d_01.html |archive-date=7 September 2007 |url-status=dead}}</ref>
==Conservation== The [[:en:Cultural Affairs Agency|Cultural Affairs Agency]] of Japan is{{As of? |date=February 2024}} considering taking apart the stone chamber and reassembling it elsewhere to prevent further deterioration to its wall paintings. A painting called ''Asuka Bijin'', or "beautiful women", is one of the [[:en:mural|mural]]s in the tomb facing deterioration. The unusual preservation method is being considered because the tomb's current situation makes it impossible to prevent further damage and stop the spread of [[:en:Mold (fungus)|mold]].
Unlike the [[Kitora Tomb]], in Asuka removing pieces of the Takamatsuzuka wall [[:en:Plaster|Plaster]] and reinforcing them for conservation appears difficult because the plaster has numerous tiny cracks.{{Citation needed|date=June 2022}}
==Comparison== In 2012, similar mural was found in a tomb in [[:en:Mongolia|Mongolia]]. The round mound, thought to be built by [[:en:Göktürks|Göktürks]] in the 7th century, was excavated by Mongolian and Kazakh researchers. The mural depicts Azure Dragon and White Tiger with a procession of Chinese and [[:en:Sogdians|Sogdian]], and other Caucasoid traders.

# [[:en:Prince Osakabe|Prince Osakabe]] (d. 705), a son of [[:en:Emperor Tenmu|Emperor Tenmu]] # [[:en:Prince Yuge|Prince Yuge]] (d. 699), also a son of Emperor Tenmu # [[:en:Prince Takechi|Prince Takechi]] ({{circa|654}} – 696), also a son of Emperor Temmu, general of [[:en:Jinshin War|Jinshin War]], [[:en:Daijō Daijin|Daijō Daijin]] # Isonokami Ason Maro (640–717), a descendant of [[Mononobe clan]] and in charge of [[:en:Fujiwara-kyō|Fujiwara-kyō]] after the capital was moved to [[Heijō-kyō]] # [[:en:Kudara no Konikishi|Kudara no Konikishi Zenkō]] (617–700), a son of the last king of [[:en:Baekje|Baekje]], one of the [[:en:Three Kingdoms of Korea|Three Kingdoms of Korea]].<ref>{{cite web | url= http://www.asukanet.gr.jp/asukahome/ASUKA2/TAKAMATUTUKA/takamatutuka.html | work = Asuka | title = Takamatuzuka | publisher = Asuka net | place = [[:en:Japan | JP]]}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url= http://www.nara-np.co.jp/special/takamatu/vol_02d_01.html |title=特集 高松塚光源 |website= Nara-np | place = JP |access-date= 13 January 2022 | language = ja |archive-url= https://web.archive.org/web/20070907112805/http://www.nara-np.co.jp/special/takamatu/vol_02d_01.html |archive-date=7 September 2007 |url-status=dead}}</ref>

A painting called ''Asuka Bijin'', or "beautiful women", is one of the [[:en:mural|mural]]s in the tomb facing deterioration. The unusual preservation method is being considered because the tomb's current situation makes it impossible to prevent further damage and stop the spread of [[:en:Mold (fungus)|mold]].
Unlike the [[Kitora Tomb]], in Asuka removing pieces of the Takamatsuzuka wall [[:en:Plaster|Plaster]] and reinforcing them for conservation appears difficult because the plaster has numerous tiny cracks.{{Citation needed|date=June 2022}}
==Comparison== In 2012, similar mural was found in a tomb in [[:en:Mongolia|Mongolia]]. The round mound, thought to be built by [[:en:Göktürks|Göktürks]] in the 7th century, was excavated by Mongolian and Kazakh researchers. The mural depicts Azure Dragon and White Tiger with a procession of Chinese and [[:en:Sogdians|Sogdian]], and other Caucasoid traders.
== Gallery == <gallery> File:Takamatsuzuka Asuka Bijin.jpg|A detail of the ''Asuka Bijin'' mural in the tomb File:高松塚古墳壁画 (複製).JPG|A reproduction of the murals at [[:en:Kansai University|Kansai University]] Museum File:Asuka Byako.JPEG|A painting of a [[:en:White Tiger (mythology)|''byakko'']] in the tomb </gallery>
==See also== *[[:en:Detachment of wall paintings|Detachment of wall paintings]] *[[:en:en:List of National Treasures of Japan (paintings)|en:List of National Treasures of Japan (paintings)]] *[[:en:List of Special Places of Scenic Beauty, Special Historic Sites and Special Natural Monuments|List of Special Places of Scenic Beauty, Special Historic Sites and Special Natural Monuments]] *[[:en:Asuka-Fujiwara|Asuka-Fujiwara]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Takamatsuzuka Kofun]][[de:Takamatsuzuka Kofun]][[en:Takamatsuzuka Tomb]][[fa:مقبره تاکاماتسوکا]][[fr:Kofun de Takamatsuzuka]][[it:Takamatsuzuka]][[ja:高松塚古墳]][[ko:다카마쓰 고분]][[ml:തക്കമത്സുക ശവകുടീരം]][[pl:Takamatsuzuka]][[zh:高松塚古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|image=File:Takamatsuzuka 2014.JPG|location={{ill|Asuka|WD=Q752397}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.46222222|N|135.80647222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles={{ill|circular kofun|WD=Q11394747}}|part_of={{ill|Asuka Historical National Government Park|WD=Q4811855}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q1039675]] Wikidata name: Takamatsuzuka Tomb [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:44, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:高松塚古墳.jpg|thumb|Takamatsuzuka Tomb]] The {{nihongo|'''Takamatsuzuka Tomb'''|高松塚古墳|''Takamatsuzuka Kofun''}} is an ancient {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular tomb|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} in [[:en:Asuka, Nara|Asuka village]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]].
==History== The tomb is thought to have been built at some time between the end of the 7th century and the beginning of the 8th century. It was accidentally discovered by a local farmer in the 1960s. ==Description== [[File:Takamat1.jpg|thumb|A mural of women on the western wall of the tomb]] The mound of the tomb was built of alternating layers of [[:en:Clay|Clay]] and [[:en:sand|sand]]. It is about {{convert |16|m}} in diameter and {{convert|5|m}} high. Excavation yielded a burial chamber with painted [[:en:fresco|fresco]] wall paintings of [[:en:Courtier|Courtier]]s in [[:en:Goguryeo|Goguryeo]]-style garb. The paintings are in full color with red, blue, gold, and silver foil representing four male followers and four serving maidens together with the [[:en:Azure Dragon (Chinese constellation) |Azure Dragon]], [[:en:Black Tortoise (Chinese constellation)|Black Tortoise]], [[:en:White Tiger (Chinese constellation)|White Tiger]], and [[:en:Vermilion Bird (Chinese constellation)|Vermilion Bird]] groups of stars. The paintings are [[:en:Cultural Property (Japan)|designated]] a [[:en:National Treasure (Japan)|National Treasure]].
For whom the tomb was built is unknown, but the decorations suggest it is for a member of the Japanese royal family or a high-Ranking nobleman. Candidates include:
# [[:en:Prince Osakabe|Prince Osakabe]] (d. 705), a son of [[:en:Emperor Tenmu|Emperor Tenmu]] # [[:en:Prince Yuge|Prince Yuge]] (d. 699), also a son of Emperor Tenmu # [[:en:Prince Takechi|Prince Takechi]] ({{circa|654}} – 696), also a son of Emperor Temmu, general of [[:en:Jinshin War|Jinshin War]], [[:en:Daijō Daijin|Daijō Daijin]] # Isonokami Ason Maro (640–717), a descendant of [[Mononobe clan]] and in charge of [[:en:Fujiwara-kyō|Fujiwara-kyō]] after the capital was moved to [[Heijō-kyō]] # [[:en:Kudara no Konikishi|Kudara no Konikishi Zenkō]] (617–700), a son of the last king of [[:en:Baekje|Baekje]], one of the [[:en:Three Kingdoms of Korea|Three Kingdoms of Korea]].<ref>{{cite web | url= http://www.asukanet.gr.jp/asukahome/ASUKA2/TAKAMATUTUKA/takamatutuka.html | work = Asuka | title = Takamatuzuka | publisher = Asuka net | place = [[:en:Japan | JP]]}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url= http://www.nara-np.co.jp/special/takamatu/vol_02d_01.html |title=特集 高松塚光源 |website= Nara-np | place = JP |access-date= 13 January 2022 | language = ja |archive-url= https://web.archive.org/web/20070907112805/http://www.nara-np.co.jp/special/takamatu/vol_02d_01.html |archive-date=7 September 2007 |url-status=dead}}</ref>
==Conservation== The [[:en:Cultural Affairs Agency|Cultural Affairs Agency]] of Japan is{{As of? |date=February 2024}} considering taking apart the stone chamber and reassembling it elsewhere to prevent further deterioration to its wall paintings. A painting called ''Asuka Bijin'', or "beautiful women", is one of the [[:en:mural|mural]]s in the tomb facing deterioration. The unusual preservation method is being considered because the tomb's current situation makes it impossible to prevent further damage and stop the spread of [[:en:Mold (fungus)|mold]].
Unlike the [[Kitora Tomb]], in Asuka removing pieces of the Takamatsuzuka wall [[:en:Plaster|Plaster]] and reinforcing them for conservation appears difficult because the plaster has numerous tiny cracks.{{Citation needed|date=June 2022}}
==Comparison== In 2012, similar mural was found in a tomb in [[:en:Mongolia|Mongolia]]. The round mound, thought to be built by [[:en:Göktürks|Göktürks]] in the 7th century, was excavated by Mongolian and Kazakh researchers. The mural depicts Azure Dragon and White Tiger with a procession of Chinese and [[:en:Sogdians|Sogdian]], and other Caucasoid traders.
== Gallery == <gallery> File:Takamatsuzuka Asuka Bijin.jpg|A detail of the ''Asuka Bijin'' mural in the tomb File:高松塚古墳壁画 (複製).JPG|A reproduction of the murals at [[:en:Kansai University|Kansai University]] Museum File:Asuka Byako.JPEG|A painting of a [[:en:White Tiger (mythology)|''byakko'']] in the tomb </gallery>
==See also== *[[:en:Detachment of wall paintings|Detachment of wall paintings]] *[[:en:en:List of National Treasures of Japan (paintings)|en:List of National Treasures of Japan (paintings)]] *[[:en:List of Special Places of Scenic Beauty, Special Historic Sites and Special Natural Monuments|List of Special Places of Scenic Beauty, Special Historic Sites and Special Natural Monuments]] *[[:en:Asuka-Fujiwara|Asuka-Fujiwara]]

The paintings are [[:en:Cultural Property (Japan)|designated]] a [[:en:National Treasure (Japan)|National Treasure]].
For whom the tomb was built is unknown, but the decorations suggest it is for a member of the Japanese royal family or a high-Ranking nobleman. Candidates include:
# [[:en:Prince Osakabe|Prince Osakabe]] (d. 705), a son of [[:en:Emperor Tenmu|Emperor Tenmu]] # [[:en:Prince Yuge|Prince Yuge]] (d. 699), also a son of Emperor Tenmu # [[:en:Prince Takechi|Prince Takechi]] ({{circa|654}} – 696), also a son of Emperor Temmu, general of [[:en:Jinshin War|Jinshin War]], [[:en:Daijō Daijin|Daijō Daijin]] # Isonokami Ason Maro (640–717), a descendant of [[Mononobe clan]] and in charge of [[:en:Fujiwara-kyō|Fujiwara-kyō]] after the capital was moved to [[Heijō-kyō]] # [[:en:Kudara no Konikishi|Kudara no Konikishi Zenkō]] (617–700), a son of the last king of [[:en:Baekje|Baekje]], one of the [[:en:Three Kingdoms of Korea|Three Kingdoms of Korea]].<ref>{{cite web | url= http://www.asukanet.gr.jp/asukahome/ASUKA2/TAKAMATUTUKA/takamatutuka.html | work = Asuka | title = Takamatuzuka | publisher = Asuka net | place = [[:en:Japan | JP]]}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url= http://www.nara-np.co.jp/special/takamatu/vol_02d_01.html |title=特集 高松塚光源 |website= Nara-np | place = JP |access-date= 13 January 2022 | language = ja |archive-url= https://web.archive.org/web/20070907112805/http://www.nara-np.co.jp/special/takamatu/vol_02d_01.html |archive-date=7 September 2007 |url-status=dead}}</ref>
==Conservation== The [[:en:Cultural Affairs Agency|Cultural Affairs Agency]] of Japan is{{As of? |date=February 2024}} considering taking apart the stone chamber and reassembling it elsewhere to prevent further deterioration to its wall paintings. A painting called ''Asuka Bijin'', or "beautiful women", is one of the [[:en:mural|mural]]s in the tomb facing deterioration. The unusual preservation method is being considered because the tomb's current situation makes it impossible to prevent further damage and stop the spread of [[:en:Mold (fungus)|mold]].
Unlike the [[Kitora Tomb]], in Asuka removing pieces of the Takamatsuzuka wall [[:en:Plaster|Plaster]] and reinforcing them for conservation appears difficult because the plaster has numerous tiny cracks.{{Citation needed|date=June 2022}}
==Comparison== In 2012, similar mural was found in a tomb in [[:en:Mongolia|Mongolia]]. The round mound, thought to be built by [[:en:Göktürks|Göktürks]] in the 7th century, was excavated by Mongolian and Kazakh researchers. The mural depicts Azure Dragon and White Tiger with a procession of Chinese and [[:en:Sogdians|Sogdian]], and other Caucasoid traders.
== Gallery == <gallery> File:Takamatsuzuka Asuka Bijin.jpg|A detail of the ''Asuka Bijin'' mural in the tomb File:高松塚古墳壁画 (複製).JPG|A reproduction of the murals at [[:en:Kansai University|Kansai University]] Museum File:Asuka Byako.JPEG|A painting of a [[:en:White Tiger (mythology)|''byakko'']] in the tomb </gallery>
==See also== *[[:en:Detachment of wall paintings|Detachment of wall paintings]] *[[:en:en:List of National Treasures of Japan (paintings)|en:List of National Treasures of Japan (paintings)]] *[[:en:List of Special Places of Scenic Beauty, Special Historic Sites and Special Natural Monuments|List of Special Places of Scenic Beauty, Special Historic Sites and Special Natural Monuments]] *[[:en:Asuka-Fujiwara|Asuka-Fujiwara]]

==History== The tomb is thought to have been built at some time between the end of the 7th century and the beginning of the 8th century. It was accidentally discovered by a local farmer in the 1960s. ==Description== [[File:Takamat1.jpg|thumb|A mural of women on the western wall of the tomb]] The mound of the tomb was built of alternating layers of [[:en:Clay|Clay]] and [[:en:sand|sand]]. It is about {{convert |16|m}} in diameter and {{convert|5|m}} high. Excavation yielded a burial chamber with painted [[:en:fresco|fresco]] wall paintings of [[:en:Courtier|Courtier]]s in [[:en:Goguryeo|Goguryeo]]-style garb. The paintings are in full color with red, blue, gold, and silver foil representing four male followers and four serving maidens together with the [[:en:Azure Dragon (Chinese constellation) |Azure Dragon]], [[:en:Black Tortoise (Chinese constellation)|Black Tortoise]], [[:en:White Tiger (Chinese constellation)|White Tiger]], and [[:en:Vermilion Bird (Chinese constellation)|Vermilion Bird]] groups of stars. The paintings are [[:en:Cultural Property (Japan)|designated]] a [[:en:National Treasure (Japan)|National Treasure]].

A painting called ''Asuka Bijin'', or "beautiful women", is one of the [[:en:mural|mural]]s in the tomb facing deterioration. The round mound, thought to be built by [[:en:Göktürks|Göktürks]] in the 7th century, was excavated by Mongolian and Kazakh researchers. The mural depicts Azure Dragon and White Tiger with a procession of Chinese and [[:en:Sogdians|Sogdian]], and other Caucasoid traders.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Takamatsuzuka Kofun]][[de:Takamatsuzuka Kofun]][[en:Takamatsuzuka Tomb]][[fa:مقبره تاکاماتسوکا]][[fr:Kofun de Takamatsuzuka]][[it:Takamatsuzuka]][[ja:高松塚古墳]][[ko:다카마쓰 고분]][[ml:തക്കമത്സുക ശവകുടീരം]][[pl:Takamatsuzuka]][[zh:高松塚古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Takamatsuzuka Kofun]][[de:Takamatsuzuka Kofun]][[en:Takamatsuzuka Tomb]][[fa:مقبره تاکاماتسوکا]][[fr:Kofun de Takamatsuzuka]][[it:Takamatsuzuka]][[ja:高松塚古墳]][[ko:다카마쓰 고분]][[ml:തക്കമത്സുക ശവകുടീരം]][[pl:Takamatsuzuka]][[zh:高松塚古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Takamatsuzuka Kofun]][[de:Takamatsuzuka Kofun]][[en:Takamatsuzuka Tomb]][[fa:مقبره تاکاماتسوکا]][[fr:Kofun de Takamatsuzuka]][[it:Takamatsuzuka]][[ja:高松塚古墳]][[ko:다카마쓰 고분]][[ml:തക്കമത്സുക ശവകുടീരം]][[pl:Takamatsuzuka]][[zh:高松塚古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|image=File:Takamatsuzuka 2014.JPG|location={{ill|Asuka|WD=Q752397}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.46222222|N|135.80647222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles={{ill|circular kofun|WD=Q11394747}}|part_of={{ill|Asuka Historical National Government Park|WD=Q4811855}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q1039675]] Wikidata name: Takamatsuzuka Tomb [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:44, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:高松塚古墳.jpg|thumb|Takamatsuzuka Tomb]] The {{nihongo|'''Takamatsuzuka Tomb'''|高松塚古墳|''Takamatsuzuka Kofun''}} is an ancient {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular tomb|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} in [[:en:Asuka, Nara|Asuka village]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]].
==History== The tomb is thought to have been built at some time between the end of the 7th century and the beginning of the 8th century. It was accidentally discovered by a local farmer in the 1960s. ==Description== [[File:Takamat1.jpg|thumb|A mural of women on the western wall of the tomb]] The mound of the tomb was built of alternating layers of [[:en:Clay|Clay]] and [[:en:sand|sand]]. It is about {{convert |16|m}} in diameter and {{convert|5|m}} high. Excavation yielded a burial chamber with painted [[:en:fresco|fresco]] wall paintings of [[:en:Courtier|Courtier]]s in [[:en:Goguryeo|Goguryeo]]-style garb. The paintings are in full color with red, blue, gold, and silver foil representing four male followers and four serving maidens together with the [[:en:Azure Dragon (Chinese constellation) |Azure Dragon]], [[:en:Black Tortoise (Chinese constellation)|Black Tortoise]], [[:en:White Tiger (Chinese constellation)|White Tiger]], and [[:en:Vermilion Bird (Chinese constellation)|Vermilion Bird]] groups of stars. The paintings are [[:en:Cultural Property (Japan)|designated]] a [[:en:National Treasure (Japan)|National Treasure]].
For whom the tomb was built is unknown, but the decorations suggest it is for a member of the Japanese royal family or a high-Ranking nobleman. Candidates include:
# [[:en:Prince Osakabe|Prince Osakabe]] (d. 705), a son of [[:en:Emperor Tenmu|Emperor Tenmu]] # [[:en:Prince Yuge|Prince Yuge]] (d. 699), also a son of Emperor Tenmu # [[:en:Prince Takechi|Prince Takechi]] ({{circa|654}} – 696), also a son of Emperor Temmu, general of [[:en:Jinshin War|Jinshin War]], [[:en:Daijō Daijin|Daijō Daijin]] # Isonokami Ason Maro (640–717), a descendant of [[Mononobe clan]] and in charge of [[:en:Fujiwara-kyō|Fujiwara-kyō]] after the capital was moved to [[Heijō-kyō]] # [[:en:Kudara no Konikishi|Kudara no Konikishi Zenkō]] (617–700), a son of the last king of [[:en:Baekje|Baekje]], one of the [[:en:Three Kingdoms of Korea|Three Kingdoms of Korea]].<ref>{{cite web | url= http://www.asukanet.gr.jp/asukahome/ASUKA2/TAKAMATUTUKA/takamatutuka.html | work = Asuka | title = Takamatuzuka | publisher = Asuka net | place = [[:en:Japan | JP]]}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url= http://www.nara-np.co.jp/special/takamatu/vol_02d_01.html |title=特集 高松塚光源 |website= Nara-np | place = JP |access-date= 13 January 2022 | language = ja |archive-url= https://web.archive.org/web/20070907112805/http://www.nara-np.co.jp/special/takamatu/vol_02d_01.html |archive-date=7 September 2007 |url-status=dead}}</ref>
==Conservation== The [[:en:Cultural Affairs Agency|Cultural Affairs Agency]] of Japan is{{As of? |date=February 2024}} considering taking apart the stone chamber and reassembling it elsewhere to prevent further deterioration to its wall paintings. A painting called ''Asuka Bijin'', or "beautiful women", is one of the [[:en:mural|mural]]s in the tomb facing deterioration. The unusual preservation method is being considered because the tomb's current situation makes it impossible to prevent further damage and stop the spread of [[:en:Mold (fungus)|mold]].
Unlike the [[Kitora Tomb]], in Asuka removing pieces of the Takamatsuzuka wall [[:en:Plaster|Plaster]] and reinforcing them for conservation appears difficult because the plaster has numerous tiny cracks.{{Citation needed|date=June 2022}}
==Comparison== In 2012, similar mural was found in a tomb in [[:en:Mongolia|Mongolia]]. The round mound, thought to be built by [[:en:Göktürks|Göktürks]] in the 7th century, was excavated by Mongolian and Kazakh researchers. The mural depicts Azure Dragon and White Tiger with a procession of Chinese and [[:en:Sogdians|Sogdian]], and other Caucasoid traders.
== Gallery == <gallery> File:Takamatsuzuka Asuka Bijin.jpg|A detail of the ''Asuka Bijin'' mural in the tomb File:高松塚古墳壁画 (複製).JPG|A reproduction of the murals at [[:en:Kansai University|Kansai University]] Museum File:Asuka Byako.JPEG|A painting of a [[:en:White Tiger (mythology)|''byakko'']] in the tomb </gallery>

== Gallery == <gallery> File:Takamatsuzuka Asuka Bijin.jpg|A detail of the ''Asuka Bijin'' mural in the tomb File:高松塚古墳壁画 (複製).JPG|A reproduction of the murals at [[:en:Kansai University|Kansai University]] Museum File:Asuka Byako.JPEG|A painting of a [[:en:White Tiger (mythology)|''byakko'']] in the tomb </gallery>
==See also== *[[:en:Detachment of wall paintings|Detachment of wall paintings]] *[[:en:en:List of National Treasures of Japan (paintings)|en:List of National Treasures of Japan (paintings)]] *[[:en:List of Special Places of Scenic Beauty, Special Historic Sites and Special Natural Monuments|List of Special Places of Scenic Beauty, Special Historic Sites and Special Natural Monuments]] *[[:en:Asuka-Fujiwara|Asuka-Fujiwara]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Takamatsuzuka Kofun]][[de:Takamatsuzuka Kofun]][[en:Takamatsuzuka Tomb]][[fa:مقبره تاکاماتسوکا]][[fr:Kofun de Takamatsuzuka]][[it:Takamatsuzuka]][[ja:高松塚古墳]][[ko:다카마쓰 고분]][[ml:തക്കമത്സുക ശവകുടീരം]][[pl:Takamatsuzuka]][[zh:高松塚古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Takamatsuzuka Kofun]][[de:Takamatsuzuka Kofun]][[en:Takamatsuzuka Tomb]][[fa:مقبره تاکاماتسوکا]][[fr:Kofun de Takamatsuzuka]][[it:Takamatsuzuka]][[ja:高松塚古墳]][[ko:다카마쓰 고분]][[ml:തക്കമത്സുക ശവകുടീരം]][[pl:Takamatsuzuka]][[zh:高松塚古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Takamatsuzuka Kofun]][[de:Takamatsuzuka Kofun]][[en:Takamatsuzuka Tomb]][[fa:مقبره تاکاماتسوکا]][[fr:Kofun de Takamatsuzuka]][[it:Takamatsuzuka]][[ja:高松塚古墳]][[ko:다카마쓰 고분]][[ml:തക്കമത്സുക ശവകുടീരം]][[pl:Takamatsuzuka]][[zh:高松塚古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|image=File:Takamatsuzuka 2014.JPG|location={{ill|Asuka|WD=Q752397}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.46222222|N|135.80647222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles={{ill|circular kofun|WD=Q11394747}}|part_of={{ill|Asuka Historical National Government Park|WD=Q4811855}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q1039675]] Wikidata name: Takamatsuzuka Tomb [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:44, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:高松塚古墳.jpg|thumb|Takamatsuzuka Tomb]] The {{nihongo|'''Takamatsuzuka Tomb'''|高松塚古墳|''Takamatsuzuka Kofun''}} is an ancient {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular tomb|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} in [[:en:Asuka, Nara|Asuka village]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]].
==History== The tomb is thought to have been built at some time between the end of the 7th century and the beginning of the 8th century. It was accidentally discovered by a local farmer in the 1960s. ==Description== [[File:Takamat1.jpg|thumb|A mural of women on the western wall of the tomb]] The mound of the tomb was built of alternating layers of [[:en:Clay|Clay]] and [[:en:sand|sand]]. It is about {{convert |16|m}} in diameter and {{convert|5|m}} high. Excavation yielded a burial chamber with painted [[:en:fresco|fresco]] wall paintings of [[:en:Courtier|Courtier]]s in [[:en:Goguryeo|Goguryeo]]-style garb. The paintings are in full color with red, blue, gold, and silver foil representing four male followers and four serving maidens together with the [[:en:Azure Dragon (Chinese constellation) |Azure Dragon]], [[:en:Black Tortoise (Chinese constellation)|Black Tortoise]], [[:en:White Tiger (Chinese constellation)|White Tiger]], and [[:en:Vermilion Bird (Chinese constellation)|Vermilion Bird]] groups of stars. The paintings are [[:en:Cultural Property (Japan)|designated]] a [[:en:National Treasure (Japan)|National Treasure]].
For whom the tomb was built is unknown, but the decorations suggest it is for a member of the Japanese royal family or a high-Ranking nobleman. Candidates include:
# [[:en:Prince Osakabe|Prince Osakabe]] (d. 705), a son of [[:en:Emperor Tenmu|Emperor Tenmu]] # [[:en:Prince Yuge|Prince Yuge]] (d. 699), also a son of Emperor Tenmu # [[:en:Prince Takechi|Prince Takechi]] ({{circa|654}} – 696), also a son of Emperor Temmu, general of [[:en:Jinshin War|Jinshin War]], [[:en:Daijō Daijin|Daijō Daijin]] # Isonokami Ason Maro (640–717), a descendant of [[Mononobe clan]] and in charge of [[:en:Fujiwara-kyō|Fujiwara-kyō]] after the capital was moved to [[Heijō-kyō]] # [[:en:Kudara no Konikishi|Kudara no Konikishi Zenkō]] (617–700), a son of the last king of [[:en:Baekje|Baekje]], one of the [[:en:Three Kingdoms of Korea|Three Kingdoms of Korea]].<ref>{{cite web | url= http://www.asukanet.gr.jp/asukahome/ASUKA2/TAKAMATUTUKA/takamatutuka.html | work = Asuka | title = Takamatuzuka | publisher = Asuka net | place = [[:en:Japan | JP]]}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url= http://www.nara-np.co.jp/special/takamatu/vol_02d_01.html |title=特集 高松塚光源 |website= Nara-np | place = JP |access-date= 13 January 2022 | language = ja |archive-url= https://web.archive.org/web/20070907112805/http://www.nara-np.co.jp/special/takamatu/vol_02d_01.html |archive-date=7 September 2007 |url-status=dead}}</ref>
==Conservation== The [[:en:Cultural Affairs Agency|Cultural Affairs Agency]] of Japan is{{As of? |date=February 2024}} considering taking apart the stone chamber and reassembling it elsewhere to prevent further deterioration to its wall paintings. A painting called ''Asuka Bijin'', or "beautiful women", is one of the [[:en:mural|mural]]s in the tomb facing deterioration. The unusual preservation method is being considered because the tomb's current situation makes it impossible to prevent further damage and stop the spread of [[:en:Mold (fungus)|mold]].
Unlike the [[Kitora Tomb]], in Asuka removing pieces of the Takamatsuzuka wall [[:en:Plaster|Plaster]] and reinforcing them for conservation appears difficult because the plaster has numerous tiny cracks.{{Citation needed|date=June 2022}}
==Comparison== In 2012, similar mural was found in a tomb in [[:en:Mongolia|Mongolia]]. The round mound, thought to be built by [[:en:Göktürks|Göktürks]] in the 7th century, was excavated by Mongolian and Kazakh researchers. The mural depicts Azure Dragon and White Tiger with a procession of Chinese and [[:en:Sogdians|Sogdian]], and other Caucasoid traders.
== Gallery == <gallery> File:Takamatsuzuka Asuka Bijin.jpg|A detail of the ''Asuka Bijin'' mural in the tomb File:高松塚古墳壁画 (複製).JPG|A reproduction of the murals at [[:en:Kansai University|Kansai University]] Museum File:Asuka Byako.JPEG|A painting of a [[:en:White Tiger (mythology)|''byakko'']] in the tomb </gallery>
==See also== *[[:en:Detachment of wall paintings|Detachment of wall paintings]] *[[:en:en:List of National Treasures of Japan (paintings)|en:List of National Treasures of Japan (paintings)]] *[[:en:List of Special Places of Scenic Beauty, Special Historic Sites and Special Natural Monuments|List of Special Places of Scenic Beauty, Special Historic Sites and Special Natural Monuments]] *[[:en:Asuka-Fujiwara|Asuka-Fujiwara]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Takamatsuzuka Kofun]][[de:Takamatsuzuka Kofun]][[en:Takamatsuzuka Tomb]][[fa:مقبره تاکاماتسوکا]][[fr:Kofun de Takamatsuzuka]][[it:Takamatsuzuka]][[ja:高松塚古墳]][[ko:다카마쓰 고분]][[ml:തക്കമത്സുക ശവകുടീരം]][[pl:Takamatsuzuka]][[zh:高松塚古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Takamatsuzuka Kofun]][[de:Takamatsuzuka Kofun]][[en:Takamatsuzuka Tomb]][[fa:مقبره تاکاماتسوکا]][[fr:Kofun de Takamatsuzuka]][[it:Takamatsuzuka]][[ja:高松塚古墳]][[ko:다카마쓰 고분]][[ml:തക്കമത്സുക ശവകുടീരം]][[pl:Takamatsuzuka]][[zh:高松塚古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Takamatsuzuka Kofun]][[de:Takamatsuzuka Kofun]][[en:Takamatsuzuka Tomb]][[fa:مقبره تاکاماتسوکا]][[fr:Kofun de Takamatsuzuka]][[it:Takamatsuzuka]][[ja:高松塚古墳]][[ko:다카마쓰 고분]][[ml:തക്കമത്സുക ശവകുടീരം]][[pl:Takamatsuzuka]][[zh:高松塚古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|image=File:Takamatsuzuka 2014.JPG|location={{ill|Asuka|WD=Q752397}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.46222222|N|135.80647222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles={{ill|circular kofun|WD=Q11394747}}|part_of={{ill|Asuka Historical National Government Park|WD=Q4811855}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q1039675]] Wikidata name: Takamatsuzuka Tomb [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:44, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:高松塚古墳.jpg|thumb|Takamatsuzuka Tomb]] The {{nihongo|'''Takamatsuzuka Tomb'''|高松塚古墳|''Takamatsuzuka Kofun''}} is an ancient {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular tomb|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|en|Asuka, Nara|WD=Q752397|lt=Asuka village}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}.
==History== The tomb is thought to have been built at some time between the end of the 7th century and the beginning of the 8th century. It was accidentally discovered by a local farmer in the 1960s. ==Description== [[File:Takamat1.jpg|thumb|A mural of women on the western wall of the tomb]] The mound of the tomb was built of alternating layers of {{ill|Clay|en|Clay|WD=Q42302|lt=Clay}} and {{ill|sand|en|sand|WD=Q34679|lt=sand}}. It is about {{convert |16|m}} in diameter and {{convert|5|m}} high. Excavation yielded a burial chamber with painted {{ill|fresco|en|fresco|WD=Q134194|lt=fresco}} wall paintings of {{ill|Courtier|en|Courtier|WD=Q1511216|lt=Courtier}}s in {{ill|Goguryeo|en|Goguryeo|WD=Q28370|lt=Goguryeo}}-style garb. The paintings are in full color with red, blue, gold, and silver foil representing four male followers and four serving maidens together with the [[:en:Azure Dragon (Chinese constellation) |Azure Dragon]], [[:en:Black Tortoise (Chinese constellation)|Black Tortoise]], [[:en:White Tiger (Chinese constellation)|White Tiger]], and [[:en:Vermilion Bird (Chinese constellation)|Vermilion Bird]] groups of stars. The paintings are {{ill|Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q858308|lt=designated}} a {{ill|National Treasure (Japan)|en|National Treasure (Japan)|WD=Q1139795|lt=National Treasure}}.
For whom the tomb was built is unknown, but the decorations suggest it is for a member of the Japanese royal family or a high-Ranking nobleman. Candidates include:
# {{ill|Prince Osakabe|en|Prince Osakabe|WD=Q3403344|lt=Prince Osakabe}} (d. 705), a son of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|en|Emperor Tenmu|WD=Q335181|lt=Emperor Tenmu}} # {{ill|Prince Yuge|en|Prince Yuge|WD=Q3076827|lt=Prince Yuge}} (d. 699), also a son of Emperor Tenmu # {{ill|Prince Takechi|en|Prince Takechi|WD=Q837590|lt=Prince Takechi}} ({{circa|654}} – 696), also a son of Emperor Temmu, general of {{ill|Jinshin War|en|Jinshin War|WD=Q2378252|lt=Jinshin War}}, [[:en:Daijō Daijin|Daijō Daijin]] # Isonokami Ason Maro (640–717), a descendant of [[Mononobe clan]] and in charge of {{ill|Fujiwara-kyō|en|Fujiwara-kyō|WD=Q1196381|lt=Fujiwara-kyō}} after the capital was moved to [[Heijō-kyō]] # [[:en:Kudara no Konikishi|Kudara no Konikishi Zenkō]] (617–700), a son of the last king of [[:en:Baekje|Baekje]], one of the {{ill|Three Kingdoms of Korea|en|Three Kingdoms of Korea|WD=Q165292|lt=Three Kingdoms of Korea}}.<ref>{{cite web | url= http://www.asukanet.gr.jp/asukahome/ASUKA2/TAKAMATUTUKA/takamatutuka.html | work = Asuka | title = Takamatuzuka | publisher = Asuka net | place = {{ill|Japan |en|Japan |WD=Q17|lt= JP}}}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url= http://www.nara-np.co.jp/special/takamatu/vol_02d_01.html |title=特集 高松塚光源 |website= Nara-np | place = JP |access-date= 13 January 2022 | language = ja |archive-url= https://web.archive.org/web/20070907112805/http://www.nara-np.co.jp/special/takamatu/vol_02d_01.html |archive-date=7 September 2007 |url-status=dead}}</ref>
==Conservation== The [[:en:Cultural Affairs Agency|Cultural Affairs Agency]] of Japan is{{As of? |date=February 2024}} considering taking apart the stone chamber and reassembling it elsewhere to prevent further deterioration to its wall paintings. A painting called ''Asuka Bijin'', or "beautiful women", is one of the {{ill|mural|en|mural|WD=Q219423|lt=mural}}s in the tomb facing deterioration. The unusual preservation method is being considered because the tomb's current situation makes it impossible to prevent further damage and stop the spread of [[:en:Mold (fungus)|mold]].
Unlike the [[Kitora Tomb]], in Asuka removing pieces of the Takamatsuzuka wall {{ill|Plaster|en|Plaster|WD=Q274988|lt=Plaster}} and reinforcing them for conservation appears difficult because the plaster has numerous tiny cracks.{{Citation needed|date=June 2022}}
==Comparison== In 2012, similar mural was found in a tomb in {{ill|Mongolia|en|Mongolia|WD=Q711|lt=Mongolia}}. The round mound, thought to be built by {{ill|Göktürks|en|Göktürks|WD=Q205466|lt=Göktürks}} in the 7th century, was excavated by Mongolian and Kazakh researchers. The mural depicts Azure Dragon and White Tiger with a procession of Chinese and [[:en:Sogdians|Sogdian]], and other Caucasoid traders.
== Gallery == <gallery> File:Takamatsuzuka Asuka Bijin.jpg|A detail of the ''Asuka Bijin'' mural in the tomb File:高松塚古墳壁画 (複製).JPG|A reproduction of the murals at {{ill|Kansai University|en|Kansai University|WD=Q1149393|lt=Kansai University}} Museum File:Asuka Byako.JPEG|A painting of a {{ill|White Tiger (mythology)|en|White Tiger (mythology)|WD=Q1072455|lt=''byakko''}} in the tomb </gallery>
==See also== *{{ill|Detachment of wall paintings|en|Detachment of wall paintings|WD=Q5265564|lt=Detachment of wall paintings}} *{{ill|en:List of National Treasures of Japan (paintings)|en|en:List of National Treasures of Japan (paintings)|WD=Q3245518|lt=en:List of National Treasures of Japan (paintings)}} *{{ill|List of Special Places of Scenic Beauty, Special Historic Sites and Special Natural Monuments|en|List of Special Places of Scenic Beauty, Special Historic Sites and Special Natural Monuments|WD=Q20982902|lt=List of Special Places of Scenic Beauty, Special Historic Sites and Special Natural Monuments}} *{{ill|Asuka-Fujiwara|en|Asuka-Fujiwara|WD=Q4811849|lt=Asuka-Fujiwara}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Takamatsuzuka Kofun]][[de:Takamatsuzuka Kofun]][[en:Takamatsuzuka Tomb]][[fa:مقبره تاکاماتسوکا]][[fr:Kofun de Takamatsuzuka]][[it:Takamatsuzuka]][[ja:高松塚古墳]][[ko:다카마쓰 고분]][[ml:തക്കമത്സുക ശവകുടീരം]][[pl:Takamatsuzuka]][[zh:高松塚古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Takamatsuzuka Kofun]][[de:Takamatsuzuka Kofun]][[en:Takamatsuzuka Tomb]][[fa:مقبره تاکاماتسوکا]][[fr:Kofun de Takamatsuzuka]][[it:Takamatsuzuka]][[ja:高松塚古墳]][[ko:다카마쓰 고분]][[ml:തക്കമത്സുക ശവകുടീരം]][[pl:Takamatsuzuka]][[zh:高松塚古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Takamatsuzuka Kofun]][[de:Takamatsuzuka Kofun]][[en:Takamatsuzuka Tomb]][[fa:مقبره تاکاماتسوکا]][[fr:Kofun de Takamatsuzuka]][[it:Takamatsuzuka]][[ja:高松塚古墳]][[ko:다카마쓰 고분]][[ml:തക്കമത്സുക ശവകുടീരം]][[pl:Takamatsuzuka]][[zh:高松塚古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|image=File:Takamatsuzuka 2014.JPG|location={{ill|Asuka|WD=Q752397}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.46222222|N|135.80647222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles={{ill|circular kofun|WD=Q11394747}}|part_of={{ill|Asuka Historical National Government Park|WD=Q4811855}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q1039675]] Wikidata name: Takamatsuzuka Tomb [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:44, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:高松塚古墳.jpg|thumb|Takamatsuzuka Tomb]] The {{nihongo|'''Takamatsuzuka Tomb'''|高松塚古墳|''Takamatsuzuka Kofun''}} is an ancient {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular tomb|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|en|Asuka, Nara|WD=Q752397|lt=Asuka village}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}.
==History== The tomb is thought to have been built at some time between the end of the 7th century and the beginning of the 8th century. It was accidentally discovered by a local farmer in the 1960s. ==Description== [[File:Takamat1.jpg|thumb|A mural of women on the western wall of the tomb]] The mound of the tomb was built of alternating layers of {{ill|Clay|en|Clay|WD=Q42302|lt=Clay}} and {{ill|sand|en|sand|WD=Q34679|lt=sand}}. It is about {{convert |16|m}} in diameter and {{convert|5|m}} high. Excavation yielded a burial chamber with painted {{ill|fresco|en|fresco|WD=Q134194|lt=fresco}} wall paintings of {{ill|Courtier|en|Courtier|WD=Q1511216|lt=Courtier}}s in {{ill|Goguryeo|en|Goguryeo|WD=Q28370|lt=Goguryeo}}-style garb. The paintings are in full color with red, blue, gold, and silver foil representing four male followers and four serving maidens together with the [[:en:Azure Dragon (Chinese constellation) |Azure Dragon]], [[:en:Black Tortoise (Chinese constellation)|Black Tortoise]], [[:en:White Tiger (Chinese constellation)|White Tiger]], and [[:en:Vermilion Bird (Chinese constellation)|Vermilion Bird]] groups of stars. The paintings are {{ill|Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q858308|lt=designated}} a {{ill|National Treasure (Japan)|en|National Treasure (Japan)|WD=Q1139795|lt=National Treasure}}.
For whom the tomb was built is unknown, but the decorations suggest it is for a member of the Japanese royal family or a high-Ranking nobleman. Candidates include:
# {{ill|Prince Osakabe|en|Prince Osakabe|WD=Q3403344|lt=Prince Osakabe}} (d. 705), a son of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|en|Emperor Tenmu|WD=Q335181|lt=Emperor Tenmu}} # {{ill|Prince Yuge|en|Prince Yuge|WD=Q3076827|lt=Prince Yuge}} (d. 699), also a son of Emperor Tenmu # {{ill|Prince Takechi|en|Prince Takechi|WD=Q837590|lt=Prince Takechi}} ({{circa|654}} – 696), also a son of Emperor Temmu, general of {{ill|Jinshin War|en|Jinshin War|WD=Q2378252|lt=Jinshin War}}, [[:en:Daijō Daijin|Daijō Daijin]] # Isonokami Ason Maro (640–717), a descendant of [[Mononobe clan]] and in charge of {{ill|Fujiwara-kyō|en|Fujiwara-kyō|WD=Q1196381|lt=Fujiwara-kyō}} after the capital was moved to [[Heijō-kyō]] # [[:en:Kudara no Konikishi|Kudara no Konikishi Zenkō]] (617–700), a son of the last king of [[:en:Baekje|Baekje]], one of the {{ill|Three Kingdoms of Korea|en|Three Kingdoms of Korea|WD=Q165292|lt=Three Kingdoms of Korea}}.<ref>{{cite web | url= http://www.asukanet.gr.jp/asukahome/ASUKA2/TAKAMATUTUKA/takamatutuka.html | work = Asuka | title = Takamatuzuka | publisher = Asuka net | place = {{ill|Japan |en|Japan |WD=Q17|lt= JP}}}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url= http://www.nara-np.co.jp/special/takamatu/vol_02d_01.html |title=特集 高松塚光源 |website= Nara-np | place = JP |access-date= 13 January 2022 | language = ja |archive-url= https://web.archive.org/web/20070907112805/http://www.nara-np.co.jp/special/takamatu/vol_02d_01.html |archive-date=7 September 2007 |url-status=dead}}</ref>
==Conservation== The [[:en:Cultural Affairs Agency|Cultural Affairs Agency]] of Japan is{{As of? |date=February 2024}} considering taking apart the stone chamber and reassembling it elsewhere to prevent further deterioration to its wall paintings. A painting called ''Asuka Bijin'', or "beautiful women", is one of the {{ill|mural|en|mural|WD=Q219423|lt=mural}}s in the tomb facing deterioration. The unusual preservation method is being considered because the tomb's current situation makes it impossible to prevent further damage and stop the spread of [[:en:Mold (fungus)|mold]].
Unlike the [[Kitora Tomb]], in Asuka removing pieces of the Takamatsuzuka wall {{ill|Plaster|en|Plaster|WD=Q274988|lt=Plaster}} and reinforcing them for conservation appears difficult because the plaster has numerous tiny cracks.{{Citation needed|date=June 2022}}
==Comparison== In 2012, similar mural was found in a tomb in {{ill|Mongolia|en|Mongolia|WD=Q711|lt=Mongolia}}. The round mound, thought to be built by {{ill|Göktürks|en|Göktürks|WD=Q205466|lt=Göktürks}} in the 7th century, was excavated by Mongolian and Kazakh researchers. The mural depicts Azure Dragon and White Tiger with a procession of Chinese and [[:en:Sogdians|Sogdian]], and other Caucasoid traders.
== Gallery == <gallery> File:Takamatsuzuka Asuka Bijin.jpg|A detail of the ''Asuka Bijin'' mural in the tomb File:高松塚古墳壁画 (複製).JPG|A reproduction of the murals at {{ill|Kansai University|en|Kansai University|WD=Q1149393|lt=Kansai University}} Museum File:Asuka Byako.JPEG|A painting of a {{ill|White Tiger (mythology)|en|White Tiger (mythology)|WD=Q1072455|lt=''byakko''}} in the tomb </gallery>
==See also== *{{ill|Detachment of wall paintings|en|Detachment of wall paintings|WD=Q5265564|lt=Detachment of wall paintings}} *{{ill|en:List of National Treasures of Japan (paintings)|en|en:List of National Treasures of Japan (paintings)|WD=Q3245518|lt=en:List of National Treasures of Japan (paintings)}} *{{ill|List of Special Places of Scenic Beauty, Special Historic Sites and Special Natural Monuments|en|List of Special Places of Scenic Beauty, Special Historic Sites and Special Natural Monuments|WD=Q20982902|lt=List of Special Places of Scenic Beauty, Special Historic Sites and Special Natural Monuments}} *{{ill|Asuka-Fujiwara|en|Asuka-Fujiwara|WD=Q4811849|lt=Asuka-Fujiwara}}

[[en:Takamatsuzuka Tomb]] <!--enwiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{Commons category|Takamatsuzuka Kofun}} [[de:Takamatsuzuka Kofun]] [[fr:Kofun de Takamatsuzuka]] [[it:Takamatsuzuka]] [[ja:高松塚古墳]] [[ko:다카마쓰 고분]] [[ml:തക്കമത്സുക ശവകുടീരം]] [[pl:Takamatsuzuka]] [[zh:高松塚古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q1039675}} [[Category:Buildings and structures in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:History of Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Special Historic Sites]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Takamatsuzuka Kofun]][[de:Takamatsuzuka Kofun]][[en:Takamatsuzuka Tomb]][[fa:مقبره تاکاماتسوکا]][[fr:Kofun de Takamatsuzuka]][[it:Takamatsuzuka]][[ja:高松塚古墳]][[ko:다카마쓰 고분]][[ml:തക്കമത്സുക ശവകുടീരം]][[pl:Takamatsuzuka]][[zh:高松塚古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Takamatsuzuka Kofun]][[de:Takamatsuzuka Kofun]][[en:Takamatsuzuka Tomb]][[fa:مقبره تاکاماتسوکا]][[fr:Kofun de Takamatsuzuka]][[it:Takamatsuzuka]][[ja:高松塚古墳]][[ko:다카마쓰 고분]][[ml:തക്കമത്സുക ശവകുടീരം]][[pl:Takamatsuzuka]][[zh:高松塚古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Takamatsuzuka Kofun]][[de:Takamatsuzuka Kofun]][[en:Takamatsuzuka Tomb]][[fa:مقبره تاکاماتسوکا]][[fr:Kofun de Takamatsuzuka]][[it:Takamatsuzuka]][[ja:高松塚古墳]][[ko:다카마쓰 고분]][[ml:തക്കമത്സുക ശവകുടീരം]][[pl:Takamatsuzuka]][[zh:高松塚古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|image=File:Takamatsuzuka 2014.JPG|location={{ill|Asuka|WD=Q752397}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.46222222|N|135.80647222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles={{ill|circular kofun|WD=Q11394747}}|part_of={{ill|Asuka Historical National Government Park|WD=Q4811855}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q1039675]] Wikidata name: Takamatsuzuka Tomb [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:44, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:高松塚古墳.jpg|thumb|Takamatsuzuka Tomb]] The {{nihongo|'''Takamatsuzuka Tomb'''|高松塚古墳|''Takamatsuzuka Kofun''}} is an ancient {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular tomb|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|en|Asuka, Nara|WD=Q752397|lt=Asuka village}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}.
==History== The tomb is thought to have been built at some time between the end of the 7th century and the beginning of the 8th century. It was accidentally discovered by a local farmer in the 1960s. ==Description== [[File:Takamat1.jpg|thumb|A mural of women on the western wall of the tomb]] The mound of the tomb was built of alternating layers of {{ill|Clay|en|Clay|WD=Q42302|lt=Clay}} and {{ill|sand|en|sand|WD=Q34679|lt=sand}}. It is about {{convert |16|m}} in diameter and {{convert|5|m}} high. Excavation yielded a burial chamber with painted {{ill|fresco|en|fresco|WD=Q134194|lt=fresco}} wall paintings of {{ill|Courtier|en|Courtier|WD=Q1511216|lt=Courtier}}s in {{ill|Goguryeo|en|Goguryeo|WD=Q28370|lt=Goguryeo}}-style garb. The paintings are in full color with red, blue, gold, and silver foil representing four male followers and four serving maidens together with the [[:en:Azure Dragon (Chinese constellation) |Azure Dragon]], [[:en:Black Tortoise (Chinese constellation)|Black Tortoise]], [[:en:White Tiger (Chinese constellation)|White Tiger]], and [[:en:Vermilion Bird (Chinese constellation)|Vermilion Bird]] groups of stars. The paintings are {{ill|Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q858308|lt=designated}} a {{ill|National Treasure (Japan)|en|National Treasure (Japan)|WD=Q1139795|lt=National Treasure}}.
For whom the tomb was built is unknown, but the decorations suggest it is for a member of the Japanese royal family or a high-Ranking nobleman. Candidates include:
# {{ill|Prince Osakabe|en|Prince Osakabe|WD=Q3403344|lt=Prince Osakabe}} (d. 705), a son of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|en|Emperor Tenmu|WD=Q335181|lt=Emperor Tenmu}} # {{ill|Prince Yuge|en|Prince Yuge|WD=Q3076827|lt=Prince Yuge}} (d. 699), also a son of Emperor Tenmu # {{ill|Prince Takechi|en|Prince Takechi|WD=Q837590|lt=Prince Takechi}} ({{circa|654}} – 696), also a son of Emperor Temmu, general of {{ill|Jinshin War|en|Jinshin War|WD=Q2378252|lt=Jinshin War}}, [[:en:Daijō Daijin|Daijō Daijin]] # Isonokami Ason Maro (640–717), a descendant of [[Mononobe clan]] and in charge of {{ill|Fujiwara-kyō|en|Fujiwara-kyō|WD=Q1196381|lt=Fujiwara-kyō}} after the capital was moved to [[Heijō-kyō]] # [[:en:Kudara no Konikishi|Kudara no Konikishi Zenkō]] (617–700), a son of the last king of [[:en:Baekje|Baekje]], one of the {{ill|Three Kingdoms of Korea|en|Three Kingdoms of Korea|WD=Q165292|lt=Three Kingdoms of Korea}}.<ref>{{cite web | url= http://www.asukanet.gr.jp/asukahome/ASUKA2/TAKAMATUTUKA/takamatutuka.html | work = Asuka | title = Takamatuzuka | publisher = Asuka net | place = {{ill|Japan |en|Japan |WD=Q17|lt= JP}}}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url= http://www.nara-np.co.jp/special/takamatu/vol_02d_01.html |title=特集 高松塚光源 |website= Nara-np | place = JP |access-date= 13 January 2022 | language = ja |archive-url= https://web.archive.org/web/20070907112805/http://www.nara-np.co.jp/special/takamatu/vol_02d_01.html |archive-date=7 September 2007 |url-status=dead}}</ref>
==Conservation== The [[:en:Cultural Affairs Agency|Cultural Affairs Agency]] of Japan is{{As of? |date=February 2024}} considering taking apart the stone chamber and reassembling it elsewhere to prevent further deterioration to its wall paintings. A painting called ''Asuka Bijin'', or "beautiful women", is one of the {{ill|mural|en|mural|WD=Q219423|lt=mural}}s in the tomb facing deterioration. The unusual preservation method is being considered because the tomb's current situation makes it impossible to prevent further damage and stop the spread of [[:en:Mold (fungus)|mold]].
Unlike the [[Kitora Tomb]], in Asuka removing pieces of the Takamatsuzuka wall {{ill|Plaster|en|Plaster|WD=Q274988|lt=Plaster}} and reinforcing them for conservation appears difficult because the plaster has numerous tiny cracks.{{Citation needed|date=June 2022}}
==Comparison== In 2012, similar mural was found in a tomb in {{ill|Mongolia|en|Mongolia|WD=Q711|lt=Mongolia}}. The round mound, thought to be built by {{ill|Göktürks|en|Göktürks|WD=Q205466|lt=Göktürks}} in the 7th century, was excavated by Mongolian and Kazakh researchers. The mural depicts Azure Dragon and White Tiger with a procession of Chinese and [[:en:Sogdians|Sogdian]], and other Caucasoid traders.
== Gallery == <gallery> File:Takamatsuzuka Asuka Bijin.jpg|A detail of the ''Asuka Bijin'' mural in the tomb File:高松塚古墳壁画 (複製).JPG|A reproduction of the murals at {{ill|Kansai University|en|Kansai University|WD=Q1149393|lt=Kansai University}} Museum File:Asuka Byako.JPEG|A painting of a {{ill|White Tiger (mythology)|en|White Tiger (mythology)|WD=Q1072455|lt=''byakko''}} in the tomb </gallery>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Takamatsuzuka Kofun]][[de:Takamatsuzuka Kofun]][[en:Takamatsuzuka Tomb]][[fa:مقبره تاکاماتسوکا]][[fr:Kofun de Takamatsuzuka]][[it:Takamatsuzuka]][[ja:高松塚古墳]][[ko:다카마쓰 고분]][[ml:തക്കമത്സുക ശവകുടീരം]][[pl:Takamatsuzuka]][[zh:高松塚古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Takamatsuzuka Kofun]][[de:Takamatsuzuka Kofun]][[en:Takamatsuzuka Tomb]][[fa:مقبره تاکاماتسوکا]][[fr:Kofun de Takamatsuzuka]][[it:Takamatsuzuka]][[ja:高松塚古墳]][[ko:다카마쓰 고분]][[ml:തക്കമത്സുക ശവകുടീരം]][[pl:Takamatsuzuka]][[zh:高松塚古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Takamatsuzuka Kofun]][[de:Takamatsuzuka Kofun]][[en:Takamatsuzuka Tomb]][[fa:مقبره تاکاماتسوکا]][[fr:Kofun de Takamatsuzuka]][[it:Takamatsuzuka]][[ja:高松塚古墳]][[ko:다카마쓰 고분]][[ml:തക്കമത്സുക ശവകുടീരം]][[pl:Takamatsuzuka]][[zh:高松塚古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|image=File:Takamatsuzuka 2014.JPG|location={{ill|Asuka|WD=Q752397}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.46222222|N|135.80647222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles={{ill|circular kofun|WD=Q11394747}}|part_of={{ill|Asuka Historical National Government Park|WD=Q4811855}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q1039675]] Wikidata name: Takamatsuzuka Tomb [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:44, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:高松塚古墳.jpg|thumb|Takamatsuzuka Tomb]] The {{nihongo|'''Takamatsuzuka Tomb'''|高松塚古墳|''Takamatsuzuka Kofun''}} is an ancient {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular tomb|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|en|Asuka, Nara|WD=Q752397|lt=Asuka village}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}.
==History== The tomb is thought to have been built at some time between the end of the 7th century and the beginning of the 8th century. It was accidentally discovered by a local farmer in the 1960s. ==Description== [[File:Takamat1.jpg|thumb|A mural of women on the western wall of the tomb]] The mound of the tomb was built of alternating layers of {{ill|Clay|en|Clay|WD=Q42302|lt=Clay}} and {{ill|sand|en|sand|WD=Q34679|lt=sand}}. It is about {{convert |16|m}} in diameter and {{convert|5|m}} high. Excavation yielded a burial chamber with painted {{ill|fresco|en|fresco|WD=Q134194|lt=fresco}} wall paintings of {{ill|Courtier|en|Courtier|WD=Q1511216|lt=Courtier}}s in {{ill|Goguryeo|en|Goguryeo|WD=Q28370|lt=Goguryeo}}-style garb. The paintings are in full color with red, blue, gold, and silver foil representing four male followers and four serving maidens together with the [[:en:Azure Dragon (Chinese constellation) |Azure Dragon]], [[:en:Black Tortoise (Chinese constellation)|Black Tortoise]], [[:en:White Tiger (Chinese constellation)|White Tiger]], and [[:en:Vermilion Bird (Chinese constellation)|Vermilion Bird]] groups of stars. The paintings are {{ill|Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q858308|lt=designated}} a {{ill|National Treasure (Japan)|en|National Treasure (Japan)|WD=Q1139795|lt=National Treasure}}.
For whom the tomb was built is unknown, but the decorations suggest it is for a member of the Japanese royal family or a high-Ranking nobleman. Candidates include:
# {{ill|Prince Osakabe|en|Prince Osakabe|WD=Q3403344|lt=Prince Osakabe}} (d. 705), a son of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|en|Emperor Tenmu|WD=Q335181|lt=Emperor Tenmu}} # {{ill|Prince Yuge|en|Prince Yuge|WD=Q3076827|lt=Prince Yuge}} (d. 699), also a son of Emperor Tenmu # {{ill|Prince Takechi|en|Prince Takechi|WD=Q837590|lt=Prince Takechi}} ({{circa|654}} – 696), also a son of Emperor Temmu, general of {{ill|Jinshin War|en|Jinshin War|WD=Q2378252|lt=Jinshin War}}, [[:en:Daijō Daijin|Daijō Daijin]] # Isonokami Ason Maro (640–717), a descendant of [[Mononobe clan]] and in charge of {{ill|Fujiwara-kyō|en|Fujiwara-kyō|WD=Q1196381|lt=Fujiwara-kyō}} after the capital was moved to [[Heijō-kyō]] # [[:en:Kudara no Konikishi|Kudara no Konikishi Zenkō]] (617–700), a son of the last king of [[:en:Baekje|Baekje]], one of the {{ill|Three Kingdoms of Korea|en|Three Kingdoms of Korea|WD=Q165292|lt=Three Kingdoms of Korea}}.<ref>{{cite web | url= http://www.asukanet.gr.jp/asukahome/ASUKA2/TAKAMATUTUKA/takamatutuka.html | work = Asuka | title = Takamatuzuka | publisher = Asuka net | place = {{ill|Japan |en|Japan |WD=Q17|lt= JP}}}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url= http://www.nara-np.co.jp/special/takamatu/vol_02d_01.html |title=特集 高松塚光源 |website= Nara-np | place = JP |access-date= 13 January 2022 | language = ja |archive-url= https://web.archive.org/web/20070907112805/http://www.nara-np.co.jp/special/takamatu/vol_02d_01.html |archive-date=7 September 2007 |url-status=dead}}</ref>
==Conservation== The [[:en:Cultural Affairs Agency|Cultural Affairs Agency]] of Japan is{{As of? |date=February 2024}} considering taking apart the stone chamber and reassembling it elsewhere to prevent further deterioration to its wall paintings. A painting called ''Asuka Bijin'', or "beautiful women", is one of the {{ill|mural|en|mural|WD=Q219423|lt=mural}}s in the tomb facing deterioration. The unusual preservation method is being considered because the tomb's current situation makes it impossible to prevent further damage and stop the spread of [[:en:Mold (fungus)|mold]].
Unlike the [[Kitora Tomb]], in Asuka removing pieces of the Takamatsuzuka wall {{ill|Plaster|en|Plaster|WD=Q274988|lt=Plaster}} and reinforcing them for conservation appears difficult because the plaster has numerous tiny cracks.{{Citation needed|date=June 2022}}
==Comparison== In 2012, similar mural was found in a tomb in {{ill|Mongolia|en|Mongolia|WD=Q711|lt=Mongolia}}. The round mound, thought to be built by {{ill|Göktürks|en|Göktürks|WD=Q205466|lt=Göktürks}} in the 7th century, was excavated by Mongolian and Kazakh researchers. The mural depicts Azure Dragon and White Tiger with a procession of Chinese and [[:en:Sogdians|Sogdian]], and other Caucasoid traders.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Takamatsuzuka Kofun]][[de:Takamatsuzuka Kofun]][[en:Takamatsuzuka Tomb]][[fa:مقبره تاکاماتسوکا]][[fr:Kofun de Takamatsuzuka]][[it:Takamatsuzuka]][[ja:高松塚古墳]][[ko:다카마쓰 고분]][[ml:തക്കമത്സുക ശവകുടീരം]][[pl:Takamatsuzuka]][[zh:高松塚古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Takamatsuzuka Kofun]][[de:Takamatsuzuka Kofun]][[en:Takamatsuzuka Tomb]][[fa:مقبره تاکاماتسوکا]][[fr:Kofun de Takamatsuzuka]][[it:Takamatsuzuka]][[ja:高松塚古墳]][[ko:다카마쓰 고분]][[ml:തക്കമത്സുക ശവകുടീരം]][[pl:Takamatsuzuka]][[zh:高松塚古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Takamatsuzuka Kofun]][[de:Takamatsuzuka Kofun]][[en:Takamatsuzuka Tomb]][[fa:مقبره تاکاماتسوکا]][[fr:Kofun de Takamatsuzuka]][[it:Takamatsuzuka]][[ja:高松塚古墳]][[ko:다카마쓰 고분]][[ml:തക്കമത്സുക ശവകുടീരം]][[pl:Takamatsuzuka]][[zh:高松塚古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|image=File:Takamatsuzuka 2014.JPG|location={{ill|Asuka|WD=Q752397}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.46222222|N|135.80647222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles={{ill|circular kofun|WD=Q11394747}}|part_of={{ill|Asuka Historical National Government Park|WD=Q4811855}}}} [[File:高松塚古墳.jpg|thumb|Takamatsuzuka Tomb]] The {{nihongo|'''Takamatsuzuka Tomb'''|高松塚古墳|''Takamatsuzuka Kofun''}} is an ancient {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular tomb|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|en|Asuka, Nara|WD=Q752397|lt=Asuka village}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}.
==History== The tomb is thought to have been built at some time between the end of the 7th century and the beginning of the 8th century. It was accidentally discovered by a local farmer in the 1960s. ==Description== [[File:Takamat1.jpg|thumb|A mural of women on the western wall of the tomb]] The mound of the tomb was built of alternating layers of {{ill|Clay|en|Clay|WD=Q42302|lt=Clay}} and {{ill|sand|en|sand|WD=Q34679|lt=sand}}. It is about {{convert |16|m}} in diameter and {{convert|5|m}} high. Excavation yielded a burial chamber with painted {{ill|fresco|en|fresco|WD=Q134194|lt=fresco}} wall paintings of {{ill|Courtier|en|Courtier|WD=Q1511216|lt=Courtier}}s in {{ill|Goguryeo|en|Goguryeo|WD=Q28370|lt=Goguryeo}}-style garb. The paintings are in full color with red, blue, gold, and silver foil representing four male followers and four serving maidens together with the [[:en:Azure Dragon (Chinese constellation) |Azure Dragon]], [[:en:Black Tortoise (Chinese constellation)|Black Tortoise]], [[:en:White Tiger (Chinese constellation)|White Tiger]], and [[:en:Vermilion Bird (Chinese constellation)|Vermilion Bird]] groups of stars. The paintings are {{ill|Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q858308|lt=designated}} a {{ill|National Treasure (Japan)|en|National Treasure (Japan)|WD=Q1139795|lt=National Treasure}}.
For whom the tomb was built is unknown, but the decorations suggest it is for a member of the Japanese royal family or a high-Ranking nobleman. Candidates include:
# {{ill|Prince Osakabe|en|Prince Osakabe|WD=Q3403344|lt=Prince Osakabe}} (d. 705), a son of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|en|Emperor Tenmu|WD=Q335181|lt=Emperor Tenmu}} # {{ill|Prince Yuge|en|Prince Yuge|WD=Q3076827|lt=Prince Yuge}} (d. 699), also a son of Emperor Tenmu # {{ill|Prince Takechi|en|Prince Takechi|WD=Q837590|lt=Prince Takechi}} ({{circa|654}} – 696), also a son of Emperor Temmu, general of {{ill|Jinshin War|en|Jinshin War|WD=Q2378252|lt=Jinshin War}}, [[:en:Daijō Daijin|Daijō Daijin]] # Isonokami Ason Maro (640–717), a descendant of [[Mononobe clan]] and in charge of {{ill|Fujiwara-kyō|en|Fujiwara-kyō|WD=Q1196381|lt=Fujiwara-kyō}} after the capital was moved to [[Heijō-kyō]] # [[:en:Kudara no Konikishi|Kudara no Konikishi Zenkō]] (617–700), a son of the last king of [[:en:Baekje|Baekje]], one of the {{ill|Three Kingdoms of Korea|en|Three Kingdoms of Korea|WD=Q165292|lt=Three Kingdoms of Korea}}.<ref>{{cite web | url= http://www.asukanet.gr.jp/asukahome/ASUKA2/TAKAMATUTUKA/takamatutuka.html | work = Asuka | title = Takamatuzuka | publisher = Asuka net | place = {{ill|Japan |en|Japan |WD=Q17|lt= JP}}}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url= http://www.nara-np.co.jp/special/takamatu/vol_02d_01.html |title=特集 高松塚光源 |website= Nara-np | place = JP |access-date= 13 January 2022 | language = ja |archive-url= https://web.archive.org/web/20070907112805/http://www.nara-np.co.jp/special/takamatu/vol_02d_01.html |archive-date=7 September 2007 |url-status=dead}}</ref>
==Conservation== The [[:en:Cultural Affairs Agency|Cultural Affairs Agency]] of Japan is{{As of? |date=February 2024}} considering taking apart the stone chamber and reassembling it elsewhere to prevent further deterioration to its wall paintings. A painting called ''Asuka Bijin'', or "beautiful women", is one of the {{ill|mural|en|mural|WD=Q219423|lt=mural}}s in the tomb facing deterioration. The unusual preservation method is being considered because the tomb's current situation makes it impossible to prevent further damage and stop the spread of [[:en:Mold (fungus)|mold]].
Unlike the [[Kitora Tomb]], in Asuka removing pieces of the Takamatsuzuka wall {{ill|Plaster|en|Plaster|WD=Q274988|lt=Plaster}} and reinforcing them for conservation appears difficult because the plaster has numerous tiny cracks.{{Citation needed|date=June 2022}}
==Comparison== In 2012, similar mural was found in a tomb in {{ill|Mongolia|en|Mongolia|WD=Q711|lt=Mongolia}}. The round mound, thought to be built by {{ill|Göktürks|en|Göktürks|WD=Q205466|lt=Göktürks}} in the 7th century, was excavated by Mongolian and Kazakh researchers. The mural depicts Azure Dragon and White Tiger with a procession of Chinese and [[:en:Sogdians|Sogdian]], and other Caucasoid traders.
== Gallery == <gallery> File:Takamatsuzuka Asuka Bijin.jpg|A detail of the ''Asuka Bijin'' mural in the tomb File:高松塚古墳壁画 (複製).JPG|A reproduction of the murals at {{ill|Kansai University|en|Kansai University|WD=Q1149393|lt=Kansai University}} Museum File:Asuka Byako.JPEG|A painting of a {{ill|White Tiger (mythology)|en|White Tiger (mythology)|WD=Q1072455|lt=''byakko''}} in the tomb </gallery>
==See also== *{{ill|Detachment of wall paintings|en|Detachment of wall paintings|WD=Q5265564|lt=Detachment of wall paintings}} *{{ill|en:List of National Treasures of Japan (paintings)|en|en:List of National Treasures of Japan (paintings)|WD=Q3245518|lt=en:List of National Treasures of Japan (paintings)}} *{{ill|List of Special Places of Scenic Beauty, Special Historic Sites and Special Natural Monuments|en|List of Special Places of Scenic Beauty, Special Historic Sites and Special Natural Monuments|WD=Q20982902|lt=List of Special Places of Scenic Beauty, Special Historic Sites and Special Natural Monuments}} *{{ill|Asuka-Fujiwara|en|Asuka-Fujiwara|WD=Q4811849|lt=Asuka-Fujiwara}}
==References== {{Reflist}}
==External links== {{commons Category|Takamatsuzuka_Kofun|Takamatsuzuka Tomb}} *[https://web.archive.org/web/20110305022936/http://www.pref.nara.jp/nara/kaido/eg/ko_nara/spot/k05.htm Takamatsuzuka ancient Kofun] *[http://www.japantimes.co.jp/weekly/news/nn2005/nn20050702a5.htm The Japan Times Weekly Online - Takamatsuzuka tomb to be moved] *[https://web.archive.org/web/20060503114824/http://gias.snu.ac.kr/wthong/publication/paekche/eng/hi3-5.pdf Paekche Culture in Asuka Japan] * [http://www.nabunken.go.jp/asuka/index.html Asuka Historical Museum]{{in lang|ja}}

The paintings are {{ill|Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q858308|lt=designated}} a {{ill|National Treasure (Japan)|en|National Treasure (Japan)|WD=Q1139795|lt=National Treasure}}.
For whom the tomb was built is unknown, but the decorations suggest it is for a member of the Japanese royal family or a high-Ranking nobleman. Candidates include:
# {{ill|Prince Osakabe|en|Prince Osakabe|WD=Q3403344|lt=Prince Osakabe}} (d. 705), a son of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|en|Emperor Tenmu|WD=Q335181|lt=Emperor Tenmu}} # {{ill|Prince Yuge|en|Prince Yuge|WD=Q3076827|lt=Prince Yuge}} (d. 699), also a son of Emperor Tenmu # {{ill|Prince Takechi|en|Prince Takechi|WD=Q837590|lt=Prince Takechi}} ({{circa|654}} – 696), also a son of Emperor Temmu, general of {{ill|Jinshin War|en|Jinshin War|WD=Q2378252|lt=Jinshin War}}, [[:en:Daijō Daijin|Daijō Daijin]] # Isonokami Ason Maro (640–717), a descendant of [[Mononobe clan]] and in charge of {{ill|Fujiwara-kyō|en|Fujiwara-kyō|WD=Q1196381|lt=Fujiwara-kyō}} after the capital was moved to [[Heijō-kyō]] # [[:en:Kudara no Konikishi|Kudara no Konikishi Zenkō]] (617–700), a son of the last king of [[:en:Baekje|Baekje]], one of the {{ill|Three Kingdoms of Korea|en|Three Kingdoms of Korea|WD=Q165292|lt=Three Kingdoms of Korea}}.<ref>{{cite web | url= http://www.asukanet.gr.jp/asukahome/ASUKA2/TAKAMATUTUKA/takamatutuka.html | work = Asuka | title = Takamatuzuka | publisher = Asuka net | place = {{ill|Japan |en|Japan |WD=Q17|lt= JP}}}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url= http://www.nara-np.co.jp/special/takamatu/vol_02d_01.html |title=特集 高松塚光源 |website= Nara-np | place = JP |access-date= 13 January 2022 | language = ja |archive-url= https://web.archive.org/web/20070907112805/http://www.nara-np.co.jp/special/takamatu/vol_02d_01.html |archive-date=7 September 2007 |url-status=dead}}</ref>

A painting called ''Asuka Bijin'', or "beautiful women", is one of the {{ill|mural|en|mural|WD=Q219423|lt=mural}}s in the tomb facing deterioration. The unusual preservation method is being considered because the tomb's current situation makes it impossible to prevent further damage and stop the spread of [[:en:Mold (fungus)|mold]].

The mural depicts Azure Dragon and White Tiger with a procession of Chinese and [[:en:Sogdians|Sogdian]], and other Caucasoid traders.

It is about {{convert |16|m}} in diameter and {{convert|5|m}} high. Excavation yielded a burial chamber with painted {{ill|fresco|en|fresco|WD=Q134194|lt=fresco|1=Fresco}} wall paintings of {{ill|Courtier|en|Courtier|WD=Q1511216|lt=Courtier|1=Courtier}}s in {{ill|Goguryeo|en|Goguryeo|WD=Q28370|lt=Goguryeo|1=Goguryeo}}-style garb. The paintings are {{ill|Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q858308|lt=designated|1=Cultural Property (Japan)}} a {{ill|National Treasure (Japan)|en|National Treasure (Japan)|WD=Q1139795|lt=National Treasure|1=National Treasure (Japan)}}.
For whom the tomb was built is unknown, but the decorations suggest it is for a member of the Japanese royal family or a high-Ranking nobleman. Candidates include:
# {{ill|Prince Osakabe|en|Prince Osakabe|WD=Q3403344|lt=Prince Osakabe|1=Prince Osakabe}} (d. 705), a son of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|en|Emperor Tenmu|WD=Q335181|lt=Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}} # {{ill|Prince Yuge|en|Prince Yuge|WD=Q3076827|lt=Prince Yuge|1=Prince Yuge}} (d. 699), also a son of Emperor Tenmu # {{ill|Prince Takechi|en|Prince Takechi|WD=Q837590|lt=Prince Takechi|1=Prince Takechi}} ({{circa|654}} – 696), also a son of Emperor Temmu, general of {{ill|Jinshin War|en|Jinshin War|WD=Q2378252|lt=Jinshin War|1=Jinshin War}}, [[:en:Daijō Daijin|Daijō Daijin]] # Isonokami Ason Maro (640–717), a descendant of [[Mononobe clan]] and in charge of {{ill|Fujiwara-kyō|en|Fujiwara-kyō|WD=Q1196381|lt=Fujiwara-kyō|1=Fujiwara-kyō}} after the capital was moved to [[Heijō-kyō]] # [[:en:Kudara no Konikishi|Kudara no Konikishi Zenkō]] (617–700), a son of the last king of [[:en:Baekje|Baekje]], one of the {{ill|Three Kingdoms of Korea|en|Three Kingdoms of Korea|WD=Q165292|lt=Three Kingdoms of Korea|1=Three Kingdoms of Korea}}.<ref>{{cite web | url= http://www.asukanet.gr.jp/asukahome/ASUKA2/TAKAMATUTUKA/takamatutuka.html | work = Asuka | title = Takamatuzuka | publisher = Asuka net | place = {{ill|Japan |en|Japan |WD=Q17|lt= JP|1=Japan}}}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url= http://www.nara-np.co.jp/special/takamatu/vol_02d_01.html |title=特集 高松塚光源 |website= Nara-np | place = JP |access-date= 13 January 2022 | language = ja |archive-url= https://web.archive.org/web/20070907112805/http://www.nara-np.co.jp/special/takamatu/vol_02d_01.html |archive-date=7 September 2007 |url-status=dead}}</ref>
==Conservation== The [[:en:Cultural Affairs Agency|Cultural Affairs Agency]] of Japan is{{As of? |date=February 2024}} considering taking apart the stone chamber and reassembling it elsewhere to prevent further deterioration to its wall paintings. A painting called ''Asuka Bijin'', or "beautiful women", is one of the {{ill|mural|en|mural|WD=Q219423|lt=mural|1=Mural}}s in the tomb facing deterioration. The unusual preservation method is being considered because the tomb's current situation makes it impossible to prevent further damage and stop the spread of [[:en:Mold (fungus)|mold]].
Unlike the [[Kitora Tomb]], in Asuka removing pieces of the Takamatsuzuka wall {{ill|Plaster|en|Plaster|WD=Q274988|lt=Plaster|1=Plaster}} and reinforcing them for conservation appears difficult because the plaster has numerous tiny cracks.{{Citation needed|date=June 2022}}
==Comparison== In 2012, similar mural was found in a tomb in {{ill|Mongolia|en|Mongolia|WD=Q711|lt=Mongolia|1=Mongolia}}. The round mound, thought to be built by {{ill|Göktürks|en|Göktürks|WD=Q205466|lt=Göktürks|1=Göktürks}} in the 7th century, was excavated by Mongolian and Kazakh researchers. The mural depicts Azure Dragon and White Tiger with a procession of Chinese and [[:en:Sogdians|Sogdian]], and other Caucasoid traders.
== Gallery == <gallery> File:Takamatsuzuka Asuka Bijin.jpg|A detail of the ''Asuka Bijin'' mural in the tomb File:高松塚古墳壁画 (複製).JPG|A reproduction of the murals at {{ill|Kansai University|en|Kansai University|WD=Q1149393|lt=Kansai University|1=Kansai University}} Museum File:Asuka Byako.JPEG|A painting of a {{ill|White Tiger (mythology)|en|White Tiger (mythology)|WD=Q1072455|lt=''byakko''|1=White Tiger (mythology)}} in the tomb </gallery>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Takamatsuzuka Kofun]][[de:Takamatsuzuka Kofun]][[en:Takamatsuzuka Tomb]][[fa:مقبره تاکاماتسوکا]][[fr:Kofun de Takamatsuzuka]][[it:Takamatsuzuka]][[ja:高松塚古墳]][[ko:다카마쓰 고분]][[ml:തക്കമത്സുക ശവകുടീരം]][[pl:Takamatsuzuka]][[zh:高松塚古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Takamatsuzuka Kofun]][[de:Takamatsuzuka Kofun]][[en:Takamatsuzuka Tomb]][[fa:مقبره تاکاماتسوکا]][[fr:Kofun de Takamatsuzuka]][[it:Takamatsuzuka]][[ja:高松塚古墳]][[ko:다카마쓰 고분]][[ml:തക്കമത്സുക ശവകുടീരം]][[pl:Takamatsuzuka]][[zh:高松塚古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Takamatsuzuka Kofun]][[de:Takamatsuzuka Kofun]][[en:Takamatsuzuka Tomb]][[fa:مقبره تاکاماتسوکا]][[fr:Kofun de Takamatsuzuka]][[it:Takamatsuzuka]][[ja:高松塚古墳]][[ko:다카마쓰 고분]][[ml:തക്കമത്സുക ശവകുടീരം]][[pl:Takamatsuzuka]][[zh:高松塚古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|image=File:Takamatsuzuka 2014.JPG|location={{ill|Asuka|WD=Q752397|1=Asuka, Nara}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.46222222|N|135.80647222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles={{ill|circular kofun|WD=Q11394747|1=Round Kofun}}|part_of={{ill|Asuka Historical National Government Park|WD=Q4811855|1=Asuka Historical National Government Park}}}} [[File:高松塚古墳.jpg|thumb|Takamatsuzuka Tomb]] The {{nihongo|'''Takamatsuzuka Tomb'''|高松塚古墳|''Takamatsuzuka Kofun''}} is an ancient {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular tomb|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|en|Asuka, Nara|WD=Q752397|lt=Asuka village|1=Asuka, Nara}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}.
==History== The tomb is thought to have been built at some time between the end of the 7th century and the beginning of the 8th century. It was accidentally discovered by a local farmer in the 1960s. ==Description== [[File:Takamat1.jpg|thumb|A mural of women on the western wall of the tomb]] The mound of the tomb was built of alternating layers of {{ill|Clay|en|Clay|WD=Q42302|lt=Clay|1=Clay}} and {{ill|sand|en|sand|WD=Q34679|lt=sand|1=Sand}}. It is about {{convert |16|m}} in diameter and {{convert|5|m}} high. Excavation yielded a burial chamber with painted {{ill|fresco|en|fresco|WD=Q134194|lt=fresco|1=Fresco}} wall paintings of {{ill|Courtier|en|Courtier|WD=Q1511216|lt=Courtier|1=Courtier}}s in {{ill|Goguryeo|en|Goguryeo|WD=Q28370|lt=Goguryeo|1=Goguryeo}}-style garb. The paintings are in full color with red, blue, gold, and silver foil representing four male followers and four serving maidens together with the [[:en:Azure Dragon (Chinese constellation) |Azure Dragon]], [[:en:Black Tortoise (Chinese constellation)|Black Tortoise]], [[:en:White Tiger (Chinese constellation)|White Tiger]], and [[:en:Vermilion Bird (Chinese constellation)|Vermilion Bird]] groups of stars. The paintings are {{ill|Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q858308|lt=designated|1=Cultural Property (Japan)}} a {{ill|National Treasure (Japan)|en|National Treasure (Japan)|WD=Q1139795|lt=National Treasure|1=National Treasure (Japan)}}.
For whom the tomb was built is unknown, but the decorations suggest it is for a member of the Japanese royal family or a high-Ranking nobleman. Candidates include:
# {{ill|Prince Osakabe|en|Prince Osakabe|WD=Q3403344|lt=Prince Osakabe|1=Prince Osakabe}} (d. 705), a son of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|en|Emperor Tenmu|WD=Q335181|lt=Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}} # {{ill|Prince Yuge|en|Prince Yuge|WD=Q3076827|lt=Prince Yuge|1=Prince Yuge}} (d. 699), also a son of Emperor Tenmu # {{ill|Prince Takechi|en|Prince Takechi|WD=Q837590|lt=Prince Takechi|1=Prince Takechi}} ({{circa|654}} – 696), also a son of Emperor Temmu, general of {{ill|Jinshin War|en|Jinshin War|WD=Q2378252|lt=Jinshin War|1=Jinshin War}}, [[:en:Daijō Daijin|Daijō Daijin]] # Isonokami Ason Maro (640–717), a descendant of [[Mononobe clan]] and in charge of {{ill|Fujiwara-kyō|en|Fujiwara-kyō|WD=Q1196381|lt=Fujiwara-kyō|1=Fujiwara-kyō}} after the capital was moved to [[Heijō-kyō]] # [[:en:Kudara no Konikishi|Kudara no Konikishi Zenkō]] (617–700), a son of the last king of [[:en:Baekje|Baekje]], one of the {{ill|Three Kingdoms of Korea|en|Three Kingdoms of Korea|WD=Q165292|lt=Three Kingdoms of Korea|1=Three Kingdoms of Korea}}.<ref>{{cite web | url= http://www.asukanet.gr.jp/asukahome/ASUKA2/TAKAMATUTUKA/takamatutuka.html | work = Asuka | title = Takamatuzuka | publisher = Asuka net | place = {{ill|Japan |en|Japan |WD=Q17|lt= JP|1=Japan}}}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url= http://www.nara-np.co.jp/special/takamatu/vol_02d_01.html |title=特集 高松塚光源 |website= Nara-np | place = JP |access-date= 13 January 2022 | language = ja |archive-url= https://web.archive.org/web/20070907112805/http://www.nara-np.co.jp/special/takamatu/vol_02d_01.html |archive-date=7 September 2007 |url-status=dead}}</ref>
==Conservation== The [[:en:Cultural Affairs Agency|Cultural Affairs Agency]] of Japan is{{As of? |date=February 2024}} considering taking apart the stone chamber and reassembling it elsewhere to prevent further deterioration to its wall paintings. A painting called ''Asuka Bijin'', or "beautiful women", is one of the {{ill|mural|en|mural|WD=Q219423|lt=mural|1=Mural}}s in the tomb facing deterioration. The unusual preservation method is being considered because the tomb's current situation makes it impossible to prevent further damage and stop the spread of [[:en:Mold (fungus)|mold]].
Unlike the [[Kitora Tomb]], in Asuka removing pieces of the Takamatsuzuka wall {{ill|Plaster|en|Plaster|WD=Q274988|lt=Plaster|1=Plaster}} and reinforcing them for conservation appears difficult because the plaster has numerous tiny cracks.{{Citation needed|date=June 2022}}
==Comparison== In 2012, similar mural was found in a tomb in {{ill|Mongolia|en|Mongolia|WD=Q711|lt=Mongolia|1=Mongolia}}. The round mound, thought to be built by {{ill|Göktürks|en|Göktürks|WD=Q205466|lt=Göktürks|1=Göktürks}} in the 7th century, was excavated by Mongolian and Kazakh researchers. The mural depicts Azure Dragon and White Tiger with a procession of Chinese and [[:en:Sogdians|Sogdian]], and other Caucasoid traders.
